```{r, fig.height=8, fig.width=10}
df_rotus <- t(df_rotus)
rotus.ix <- rep(NA, length(top_asvs))
long <- length(rotus.ix)
for (i in 1:long){
  rotus.ix[i] <- grep(top_asvs[i], colnames(df_rotus))
}

# normalize and create weighted average
rotus.norm <- sweep(r_otus,2,colSums(r_otus),'/')
r.wa <- t(rotus.norm) %*% w_unipcoa # weighted average

# Coordinates of biplot objects
# adding top objects then making it same length as main data (26)
end.x <- c((r.wa[rotus.ix,1]), rep(NA, 19))
end.y <- c((r.wa[rotus.ix,2]), rep(NA, 19))
start <- rep(0, 26)
# custom nudge based on viewing graph
nudge.y <- c(-.01, .02, -.02, -.01, .02, -.02, .02, rep(NA, 19))
nudge.x <- c(.08, 0, 0, -.15, .02, 0, 0, rep(NA, 19))

# names of biplot arrows
bi.names <- data.frame('Names' = c(colnames(r_otus)[rotus.ix], rep(NA, 19)))
# rename to use italics
bi.names <- bi.names %>%
  mutate(Names = str_replace(Names,
                             "Clostridium_sp",
                             "italic('Clostridium_sp')"),
         Names = str_replace(Names,
                             "Romboutsia_genus",
                             "italic('Romboutsia_genus')"),
         Names = str_replace(Names,
                             "Enterobacteriaceae_family",
                         "italic('Enterobacteriaceae_family')"),
         Names = str_replace(Names,
                             "Lachnospiraceae_UCG_genus",
                           "italic('Lachnospiraceae_UCG_genus')"),
         Names = str_replace(Names,
                             "Colidextribacter_genus",
                             "italic('Colidextribacter_genus')"),
         Names = str_replace(Names,
                             "Oscillospira_genus",
                             "italic('Oscillospira_genus')"),
         Names = str_replace(Names,
                             "Blautia_genus",
                             "italic('Blautia_genus')"))


# arrows(x0=0, x1=l.x, y0=0, y1=l.y, col='red', length=.15, lwd=1.3)
# text(l.x, l.y, labels=colnames(r_otus)[rotus.ix], col='red', pos=l.pos2, cex=1)


p <- ggplot(data=pcoa_w_uni_plotdata,
       mapping=aes(x=PC1, y=PC2, color=Group, label=Subject_Num, shape=Sex)) +
    geom_text(color="black") + 
    geom_point(alpha=0.6, size=8) +
    scale_color_manual(values=c("Before" = "mediumpurple1", "After" = "palegreen")) +
    geom_line(aes(group = Subject_Num),color="grey") +
    theme_bw() + 
    stat_ellipse(aes(group = Group), level =.95) +
    #scale_color_discrete(name="") +
    xlab(paste0("PC1 (", round(pcoa_w_uni$ProportionExplained[1]*100, 1), "%)")) +
    ylab(paste0("PC2 (", round(pcoa_w_uni$ProportionExplained[2]*100, 1), "%)")) +
    theme(text = element_text(size=16),
          legend.text=element_text(size=16),
          legend.position = c(0.1, 0.85),
          axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12),
          axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
          axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
          axis.line = element_line(colour = "black"),
          panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
          panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
          panel.border = element_blank(),
          panel.background = element_blank())

#p

p + geom_segment(aes(x = start, y = start, xend = end.x, yend = end.y),
                 arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.3, "cm"), ends='last'),
                 colour = 'red',
                 lineend = 'round',
                 linejoin = 'round') +
  geom_text_repel(aes(x= end.x, y = end.y, label = bi.names$Names),
            color = 'red',
            size = 5,
            nudge_y = nudge.y,
            nudge_x = nudge.x,
            parse = TRUE)

#ggsave(file="figures/asv_pcoa.png", width=10, height=8)
ggsave(file="figures/asv_pcoa.svg", width=10, height=8)
```



#### PCoA w Genus biplot
```{r}
tax_eubac <- read_csv("data/top_ANCOMgenus_ASVs.csv")
metadata <- read_q2metadata("data/aya_meta_costarica.tsv")
metadata <- filter(metadata, Consumed_Aya == "yes")
rownames(metadata) <- metadata$SampleID

# make nicknamed rarefied table
rarefied_table <-read_qza("core-diversity-results-cr/rarefied_table.qza")$data
taxonomy <- read_qza("data/taxonomy-silva.qza")$data
taxonomy_split <- read_qza("data/taxonomy-silva.qza")$data %>% parse_taxonomy()
taxonomy_split$Feature.ID <- taxonomy$Feature.ID
asvs <- rownames(rarefied_table)
taxa <- vector(length=length(asvs))
genus <- vector(length=length(asvs))

i <- 1
for (a in asvs){
    taxa[i] <- taxonomy[taxonomy$Feature.ID==a,"Taxon"]
    genus[i] <- taxonomy_split[taxonomy_split$Feature.ID==a,"Genus"]
    i <- i + 1
}

df <- data.frame(asvs, taxa, genus)
rarefied_table_tax <- rarefied_table
rownames(rarefied_table_tax) <- df$genus

pcoa_w_uni <- read_qza("core-diversity-results-cr/weighted_unifrac_pcoa_results.qza")$data

# PCoA plot with weighted UniFrac
pcoa_w_uni_plotdata <- pcoa_w_uni$Vectors
rownames(pcoa_w_uni_plotdata) <- pcoa_w_uni_plotdata$SampleID
pcoa_w_uni_plotdata <- merge(pcoa_w_uni_plotdata, metadata)
pcoa_w_uni_plotdata$Group <- ifelse(pcoa_w_uni_plotdata$Timepoint=="1",
                                    yes="Before", no="After")
pcoa_w_uni_plotdata$Group <- factor(pcoa_w_uni_plotdata$Group)
pcoa_w_uni_plotdata$Group <- fct_relevel(pcoa_w_uni_plotdata$Group, "Before", "After")

# making biplot arrows
r_otus <- rarefied_table_tax
r_otus <- t(r_otus)
r_otus <- sweep(r_otus, 1, rowSums(r_otus), '/')

w_unipcoa <- dplyr::select(pcoa_w_uni_plotdata, PC1, PC2)
w_unipcoa <- data.matrix(w_unipcoa)
rownames(w_unipcoa) <- pcoa_w_uni_plotdata$SampleID

# get indices for ANCOM results
top_asvs <- tax_eubac$asvs
df_rotus <- as.data.frame(rarefied_table)
df_rotus <- t(df_rotus)
rotus.ix <- rep(NA, length(top_asvs))
long <- length(rotus.ix)
for (i in 1:long){
  rotus.ix[i] <- grep(top_asvs[i], colnames(df_rotus))
}

# normalize and create weighted average
rotus.norm <- sweep(r_otus,2,colSums(r_otus),'/')
r.wa <- t(rotus.norm) %*% w_unipcoa # weighted average

# Coordinates of biplot objects
# adding top objects then making it same length as main data (26)
end.x <- c((r.wa[rotus.ix,1]), rep(NA, 22))
end.y <- c((r.wa[rotus.ix,2]), rep(NA, 22))
start <- rep(0, 26)

# add an averaged arrow 
# which represents the Eubacterium (lachnospira) eligens genus
avg.end.x <- rep(NA, 26)
avg.end.y <- rep(NA, 26)
avg.end.x[1] <- mean(end.x[1:4])
avg.end.y[1] <- mean(end.y[1:4])

# custom nudge based on viewing graph
nudge.x <- c(-0.05, rep(NA, 25))
nudge.y <- c(-0.002, rep(NA, 25))


# names of biplot arrows
# which I don't use unless i show each arrow name
bi.names <- data.frame('Names' = c(colnames(r_otus)[rotus.ix], rep(NA, 22)))
# rename using BLAST results
bi.names$Names <- ifelse(bi.names$Names == "[Eubacterium]_eligens_group",
                         bi.names$Names <- "italic('Eubacterium_eligens_group')",
                         bi.names$Names <- NA)



p <- ggplot(data=pcoa_w_uni_plotdata,
       mapping=aes(x=PC1, y=PC2, color=Group, label=Subject_Num, shape=Sex)) +
    geom_text(color="black") + 
    geom_point(alpha=0.6, size=8) +
    scale_color_manual(values=c("Before" = "mediumpurple1", "After" = "palegreen")) +
    geom_line(aes(group = Subject_Num),color="grey") +
    theme_bw() + 
    stat_ellipse(aes(group = Group), level =.95) +
    #scale_color_discrete(name="") +
    xlab(paste0("PC1 (", round(pcoa_w_uni$ProportionExplained[1]*100, 1), "%)")) +
    ylab(paste0("PC2 (", round(pcoa_w_uni$ProportionExplained[2]*100, 1), "%)")) +
    theme(text = element_text(size=16),
          legend.text=element_text(size=16),
          legend.position = c(0.1, 0.85),
          axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12),
          axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
          axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
          axis.line = element_line(colour = "black"),
          panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
          panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
          panel.border = element_blank(),
          panel.background = element_blank()) +
  geom_segment(aes(x = start, y = start, xend = end.x, yend = end.y),
                 arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.3, "cm"), ends='last'),
                 colour = 'pink',
                 lineend = 'round',
                 linejoin = 'round')

p + geom_segment(aes(x = start, y = start, xend = avg.end.x, yend = avg.end.y),
                 arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.3, "cm"), ends='last'),
                 colour = 'red',
                 lineend = 'round',
                 linejoin = 'round',
                 linewidth = 1) +
  geom_text_repel(aes(x= avg.end.x, y = avg.end.y, 
                      label = "italic('Eubacterium_eligens_group')"),
                  color = 'red',
                  size = 6,
                  nudge_y = nudge.y,
                  nudge_x = nudge.x,
                  segment.color = 'transparent',
                  parse = TRUE)

#ggsave(file="figures/genus_pcoa.png", width=10, height=8)
```

#### PCoA w Phylum biplot
Run ANCOM Phylum level first to get res_top
```{r}
metadata <- read_q2metadata("data/aya_meta_costarica.tsv")
metadata <- filter(metadata, Consumed_Aya == "yes")
rownames(metadata) <- metadata$SampleID

# make nicknamed rarefied table
rarefied_table <-read_qza("core-diversity-results-cr/rarefied_table.qza")$data
taxonomy <- read_qza("data/taxonomy-silva.qza")$data
taxonomy_split <- read_qza("data/taxonomy-silva.qza")$data %>% parse_taxonomy()
taxonomy_split$Feature.ID <- taxonomy$Feature.ID
asvs <- rownames(rarefied_table)
taxa <- vector(length=length(asvs))
phylum <- vector(length=length(asvs))

i <- 1
for (a in asvs){
    taxa[i] <- taxonomy[taxonomy$Feature.ID==a,"Taxon"]
    phylum[i] <- taxonomy_split[taxonomy_split$Feature.ID==a,"Phylum"]
    i <- i + 1
}

df <- data.frame(asvs, taxa, phylum)
rarefied_table_tax <- rarefied_table
rownames(rarefied_table_tax) <- df$phylum

# PCoA plot with weighted UniFrac
pcoa_w_uni <- read_qza("core-diversity-results-cr/weighted_unifrac_pcoa_results.qza")$data
pcoa_w_uni_plotdata <- pcoa_w_uni$Vectors
rownames(pcoa_w_uni_plotdata) <- pcoa_w_uni_plotdata$SampleID
pcoa_w_uni_plotdata <- merge(pcoa_w_uni_plotdata, metadata)
pcoa_w_uni_plotdata$Group <- ifelse(pcoa_w_uni_plotdata$Timepoint=="1",
                                    yes="Before", no="After")
pcoa_w_uni_plotdata$Group <- factor(pcoa_w_uni_plotdata$Group)
pcoa_w_uni_plotdata$Group <- fct_relevel(pcoa_w_uni_plotdata$Group, "Before", "After")

# making biplot arrows
r_otus <- rarefied_table_tax
r_otus <- t(r_otus)
r_otus <- sweep(r_otus, 1, rowSums(r_otus), '/')

w_unipcoa <- dplyr::select(pcoa_w_uni_plotdata, PC1, PC2)
w_unipcoa <- data.matrix(w_unipcoa)
rownames(w_unipcoa) <- pcoa_w_uni_plotdata$SampleID

# prep data for pulling columns
df_rotus <- as.data.frame(rarefied_table)
df_rotus <- t(df_rotus)
# normalize and create weighted average
rotus.norm <- sweep(r_otus,2,colSums(r_otus),'/')
r.wa <- t(rotus.norm) %*% w_unipcoa # weighted average

# verrucomicrobiota
# get indices for ANCOM results
top_asvs_v <- tax_ver$asvs
rotus.ix_v <- rep(NA, length(top_asvs_v))
long <- length(rotus.ix_v)
for (i in 1:long){
  rotus.ix_v[i] <- grep(top_asvs_v[i], colnames(df_rotus))
}

# Coordinates of biplot objects
end.x_v <- r.wa[rotus.ix_v,1]
end.y_v <- r.wa[rotus.ix_v,2]

# now with actinobacteriota
top_asvs_a <- tax_act$asvs
rotus.ix_a <- rep(NA, length(top_asvs_a))
long <- length(rotus.ix_a)
for (i in 1:long){
  rotus.ix_a[i] <- grep(top_asvs_a[i], colnames(df_rotus))
}

end.x_a <- r.wa[rotus.ix_a,1]
end.y_a <- r.wa[rotus.ix_a,2]

# get average arrow for verruco and actino
# as a side note they're not alphabetical, they're going in order of p-value
avg.end.x <- rep(NA, 26)
avg.end.y <- rep(NA, 26)
start <- rep(0, 26)

# put them in vectors for plotting
avg.end.x[1] <- mean(end.x_v)
avg.end.y[1] <- mean(end.y_v)
avg.end.x[2] <- mean(end.x_a)
avg.end.y[2] <- mean(end.y_a)

# custom nudge for labels based on viewing graph
nudge.x <- c(0.15, 0, rep(NA, 24))
nudge.y <- c(0.002, 0.03, rep(NA, 24))

# biplot arrow names
bi.names <- data.frame('Names' = c("Verrucomicrobiota",
                                   "Actinobacteriota",
                                   rep(NA, 24)))

ggplot(data=pcoa_w_uni_plotdata,
       mapping=aes(x=PC1, y=PC2, color=Group, label=Subject_Num, shape=Sex)) +
    geom_text(color="black") + 
    geom_point(alpha=0.6, size=8) +
    scale_color_manual(values=c("Before" = "mediumpurple1", "After" = "palegreen")) +
    geom_line(aes(group = Subject_Num),color="grey") +
    theme_bw() + 
    stat_ellipse(aes(group = Group), level =.95) +
    #scale_color_discrete(name="") +
    xlab(paste0("PC1 (", round(pcoa_w_uni$ProportionExplained[1]*100, 1), "%)")) +
    ylab(paste0("PC2 (", round(pcoa_w_uni$ProportionExplained[2]*100, 1), "%)")) +
    theme(text = element_text(size=16),
          legend.text=element_text(size=16),
          legend.position = c(0.1, 0.85),
          axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12),
          axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
          axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
          axis.line = element_line(colour = "black"),
          panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
          panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
          panel.border = element_blank(),
          panel.background = element_blank()) +
 geom_segment(aes(x = start, y = start, xend = avg.end.x, yend = avg.end.y),
                 arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.3, "cm"), ends='last'),
                 colour = 'red',
                 lineend = 'round',
                 linejoin = 'round',
                 linewidth = 1) +
  geom_text_repel(aes(x= avg.end.x, y = avg.end.y, 
                      label = bi.names$Names),
                  color = 'red',
                  size = 6,
                  nudge_y = nudge.y,
                  nudge_x = nudge.x,
                  segment.color = 'transparent',
                  parse = TRUE)

#ggsave(file="figures/phylum_pcoa.svg", width=10, height=8)

```


# MaAsLin2 Correlations
Added 4/23/26.
```{r}
# Prep data
metadata <- read_q2metadata("data/aya_meta_costarica.tsv")
metadata <- filter(metadata, Consumed_Aya == "yes")
rownames(metadata) <- metadata$SampleID

meta_no17 <- filter(metadata, metadata$Subject_Num != "17")
meta_no17$Subject_Num <- as.character(meta_no17$Subject_Num)
meta_no17$Subject_Num <- as.factor(meta_no17$Subject_Num)


rarefied_table <-read_qza("core-diversity-results-cr/rarefied_table.qza")$data
rare_no17 <- as.data.frame(rarefied_table)
rare_no17 <- dplyr::select(rare_no17, -`17-T1`, -`17-T2`)
rare_no17 <- as.matrix(rare_no17)


fit <- Maaslin2(
  input_data = rare_no17,
  input_metadata = meta_no17,
  output = "maaslin_out/PCL5",
  fixed_effects = c("PCL5_sum_1_2")
)

fit <- Maaslin2(
  input_data = rare_no17,
  input_metadata = meta_no17,
  output = "maaslin_out/Neuroticism",
  fixed_effects = c("Neuroticism")
)


```



# Volatility
```{r}
w_uni <- read_qza("core-diversity-results-cr17/weighted_unifrac_distance_matrix.qza")$data
u_uni <- read_qza("core-diversity-results-cr17/unweighted_unifrac_distance_matrix.qza")$data

metadata <- read_q2metadata("data/aya_meta_costarica.tsv")
# It's secretly keeping subject IDs unless I get rid of the factor levels
metadata$Subject_Num <- as.character(metadata$Subject_Num)
metadata$SampleID <- as.character(metadata$SampleID)
metadata <- filter(metadata, 
                   Consumed_Aya == "yes")
metadata <- metadata[, colSums(is.na(metadata))==0]
metadata$Subject_Num <- as.factor(metadata$Subject_Num)
# drops income columns

volatility <- matrix(ncol=4)

for (sub in metadata$Subject_Num){
    sub.df <- metadata[metadata$Subject_Num==sub,]
    times <- sub.df$Timepoint
    for (i in 2:length(times)){
        id <- sub.df$SampleID[i]
        prev.id <- sub.df$SampleID[i-1]
        dist.w <- dist_get(w_uni, id, prev.id)
        dist.u <- dist_get(u_uni, id, prev.id)
        volatility <- rbind(volatility,
                            c(sub, times[i], dist.w, dist.u))
    }
}

volatility <- volatility[2:nrow(volatility),]
volatility <- as.data.frame(volatility)

colnames(volatility) <- c("Subject", "Timepoint", "Weighted UniFrac from previous", 
                          "Unweighted UniFrac from previous")

volatility$`Weighted UniFrac from previous` <- as.numeric(volatility$`Weighted UniFrac from previous`)
volatility$`Unweighted UniFrac from previous` <- as.numeric(volatility$`Unweighted UniFrac from previous`)
volatility$Timepoint <- as.numeric(volatility$Timepoint)

metadata$W_uni <- volatility$`Weighted UniFrac from previous`
metadata$U_uni <- volatility$`Unweighted UniFrac from previous`

# Get differences of psych data
# adapted from correlation code section
# FFM... or OCEAN
df <- metadata
# FFM... or OCEAN
df$O_Change <- NA # openness
df$C_Change <- NA # conscientiousness
df$E_Change <- NA # extraversion
df$A_Change <- NA # agreeableness
df$N_Change <- NA # neuroticism

# PCL5 (PTSD score)
df$PCL_Change <- NA

psy_measures <- c("Openness",
                  "Conscientiousness",
                  "Extraversion",
                  "Agreeableness",
                  "Neuroticism",
                  "PCL5_sum_1_2")

evens <- seq(2,24, by = 2)
for (x in evens){
  for (psy in psy_measures){
    before <- df[x,psy]
    after <- df[x-1, psy]
    change <- after-before
    
    if (psy == "Openness"){
      df$O_Change[x-1] <- change
      df$O_Change[x] <- change
    }
    
    if (psy == "Conscientiousness"){
    df$C_Change[x-1] <- change
    df$C_Change[x] <- change
    }

    if (psy == "Extraversion"){
    df$E_Change[x-1] <- change
    df$E_Change[x] <- change
    }
    
    if (psy == "Agreeableness"){
    df$A_Change[x-1] <- change
    df$A_Change[x] <- change
    }
    
    if (psy == "Neuroticism"){
    df$N_Change[x-1] <- change
    df$N_Change[x] <- change
    }
    
    if (psy == "PCL5_sum_1_2"){
    df$PCL_Change[x-1] <- change
    df$PCL_Change[x] <- change
    }
  }
}


metadata <- filter(df, Timepoint == "1")

#plot(metadata$W_uni, metadata$N_diffs)
ggplot(metadata, aes(x=W_uni, y=N_Change)) +
    geom_point() +
    geom_smooth(method=lm, se=FALSE)

mod1 <- lm(W_uni ~ N_Change, data=metadata)
summary(mod1)
#plot(mod1, which = 2)


ggplot(metadata, aes(x=U_uni, y=N_Change)) +
    geom_point() +
    geom_smooth(method=lm, se=FALSE)

mod2 <- lm(U_uni ~ N_Change, data=metadata)
summary(mod2)

# Correlation tests
p_o <- cor.test(metadata$O_Change, metadata$W_uni, method="pearson")$p.value
p_c <- cor.test(metadata$C_Change, metadata$W_uni, method="pearson")$p.value
p_e <- cor.test(metadata$E_Change, metadata$W_uni, method="pearson")$p.value
p_a <- cor.test(metadata$A_Change, metadata$W_uni, method="pearson")$p.value
p_n <- cor.test(metadata$N_Change, metadata$W_uni, method="pearson")$p.value
p_p <- cor.test(metadata$PCL_Change, metadata$W_uni, method="pearson")$p.value

p_ou <- cor.test(metadata$O_Change, metadata$U_uni, method="pearson")$p.value
p_cu <- cor.test(metadata$C_Change, metadata$U_uni, method="pearson")$p.value
p_eu <- cor.test(metadata$E_Change, metadata$U_uni, method="pearson")$p.value
p_au <- cor.test(metadata$A_Change, metadata$U_uni, method="pearson")$p.value
p_nu <- cor.test(metadata$N_Change, metadata$U_uni, method="pearson")$p.value
p_pu <- cor.test(metadata$PCL_Change, metadata$U_uni, method="pearson")$p.value


```



# Taxa bar plot 
Code developed by John Sterrett.

## Load data
```{r}
pseq <- qiime2R::qza_to_phyloseq(feature="data/cr-table.qza",
                                 taxonomy="data/taxonomy-silva.qza",
                                 metadata="data/aya_meta_costarica.tsv")



taxonomy.table <- pseq %>% tax_table() %>% as.data.frame()
taxonomy.table$Phylum[taxonomy.table$Phylum=="Firmicutes"] <- "Bacillota"
taxonomy.table$Phylum[taxonomy.table$Phylum=="Proteobacteria"] <- "Pseudomonadota"

tax_table(pseq) <- as.matrix(taxonomy.table)
```

## Microshades Function {.tabset}
```{r}
subgroup <- "Family"

my_plot_microshades <- function (mdf_group,
                                cdf,
                                group_label = "Phylum Genus",
                                x = "Sample",
                                y = "Abundance")
{
  if (class(mdf_group) != "data.frame")
  {
    stop("mdf_group argument must be a data frame")
  }

  if(any(is.na(cdf$hex) | is.na(cdf$group)))
  {
    stop("cdf does not contain complete color information - missing hex or group info")
  }


  plot <- mdf_group %>%
    ggplot(aes_string(x = x, y = y), fill = group_label) +
    scale_fill_manual(name = group_label,
                      values = cdf$hex,
                      breaks = cdf$group) +
    scale_colour_manual(name = group_label,
                        values = cdf$hex,
                        breaks = cdf$group) +
    geom_bar(aes(color=group, fill=group), stat="identity", position="stack") +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

  plot
}

make_microshades_phylum <- function(pseq, subgroup){
    # prep the microshades colors
    mdf_prep <- prep_mdf(pseq, subgroup_level=subgroup)
    # sort the phylum names
    phylum_table <- tax_glom(pseq, taxrank="Phylum", ) %>% otu_table()
    phyla.otunames <- rownames(phylum_table)
    
    phylums <- taxonomy.table[phyla.otunames,"Phylum"]
    
    sorted_phylums <- phylums[order(rowSums(phylum_table), decreasing=T)]
    # create the colors object
    color_objs_GP <- create_color_dfs(mdf_prep, selected_groups=sorted_phylums[5:1], 
                                      cvd = TRUE, subgroup_level=subgroup)
    # Extract
    mdf_GP <- color_objs_GP$mdf
    cdf_GP <- color_objs_GP$cdf
    # create a custom legend
    GP_legend <-custom_legend(mdf_GP, cdf_GP, 
                              legend_key_size=unit(0.4, "cm"),
                              legend_text_size=10, subgroup_level=subgroup)
    
    # plot
    plot <- my_plot_microshades(mdf_GP, cdf_GP)
    plot_1 <- plot + scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent, expand = expansion(0)) +
      theme(legend.position = "none")  +
      #theme(axis.text.x = element_blank()) +
      facet_grid(~Subject_Num, scales="free_x", space="free_x"
                 )
    
    multi <- plot_grid(plot_1, GP_legend,  rel_widths = c(1, .25))
    multi
}



```



## Plot
```{r, warning=F, echo=F, fig.height=8}
make_microshades_phylum(pseq, "Family")
ggsave(file="figures/taxa_barplot_fig.png", width=11, height=8)
ggsave(file="figures/taxa_barplot_fig.svg", width=11, height=8,
       bg="white", device = Cairo::CairoSVG)
```


# Correlation Analysis
## Psych data
```{r}
psy <- read_q2metadata("data/aya_meta_costarica.tsv")
psy <- filter(psy, Consumed_Aya == "yes", Subject_Num !=17)

hist(psy$Openness, breaks=10)
plot(psy$Timepoint, psy$Openness)

hist(psy$Conscientiousness, breaks=10)
plot(psy$Timepoint, psy$Conscientiousness)

hist(psy$Extraversion, breaks=10)
plot(psy$Timepoint, psy$Extraversion)

hist(psy$Agreeableness, breaks=10)
plot(psy$Timepoint, psy$Agreeableness)


hist(psy$Neuroticism, breaks = 10)
plot(psy$Timepoint, psy$Neuroticism)

hist(psy$PCL5_sum_1_2, breaks=10)
plot(psy$Timepoint, psy$PCL5_sum_1_2)

psy1 <- filter(psy, Timepoint == "1")
psy2 <- filter(psy, Timepoint == "2")
  
wilcox.test(psy1$Openness, 
            psy2$Openness, paired = TRUE)
# p = 0.02174

wilcox.test(psy1$Conscientiousness, 
            psy2$Conscientiousness, paired = TRUE)
# p = 0.002516

wilcox.test(psy1$Extraversion, 
            psy2$Extraversion, paired = TRUE)
# p = 0.0369

wilcox.test(psy1$Agreeableness, 
            psy2$Agreeableness, paired = TRUE)
# p = 0.00532

wilcox.test(psy1$Neuroticism, 
            psy2$Neuroticism, paired = TRUE)
# p = 0.002497


wilcox.test(psy1$PCL5_sum_1_2, 
            psy2$PCL5_sum_1_2, paired = TRUE)
# p = 0.02061

# hist(psy$Neuroticism, breaks = 10)
# 
# ggplot(psy, aes(x = Timepoint, y = PCL5_sum_1_2)) +
#   geom_point() +
#   geom_smooth(method = "lm")

# m2 <- lme(Neuroticism ~ Timepoint, random=~1|Subject_Num, data = psy)
# anova(m2)



# psy$Openness <- factor(psy$Openness, order = T)
# m <- polr(Openness ~ Timepoint, data = psy, Hess=TRUE)
# summary(m)
# ctable <- coef(summary(m))
# p <- pnorm(abs(ctable[, "t value"]), lower.tail = FALSE) * 2

pcl_1 <- ifelse(psy1$PCL5_sum_1_2 > 33,
                1,
                0)
sum(pcl_1)

pcl_2 <- ifelse(psy2$PCL5_sum_1_2 > 33,
                1,
                0)
sum(pcl_2)



```


## WHOQOL
**WHOQOL scores compare timepoint 1 to 3 months after ayahuasca, in contrast to all other data
14-T2 and 28-T2 are missing WHOQOL data
No significance in the four measures: Physical Health, Psychological Health, Social relationships, or Environment
```{r}
psy <- read_q2metadata("data/aya_meta_costarica.tsv")
psy <- filter(psy, Consumed_Aya == "yes", Subject_Num != "14")
psy <- filter(psy, Subject_Num != "28")

plot(psy$Timepoint, psy$WHOQOL_PhysHealth)
pairwise.t.test(psy$WHOQOL_PhysHealth,
                psy$Timepoint,
                p.adjust.method = 'bonf',
                paired=T)

plot(psy$Timepoint, psy$WHOQOL_PsychHealth)
pairwise.t.test(psy$WHOQOL_PsychHealth,
                psy$Timepoint,
                p.adjust.method = 'bonf',
                paired=T)

plot(psy$Timepoint, psy$WHOQOL_Social)
pairwise.t.test(psy$WHOQOL_Social,
                psy$Timepoint,
                p.adjust.method = 'bonf',
                paired=T)

plot(psy$Timepoint, psy$WHOQOL_Env)
pairwise.t.test(psy$WHOQOL_Env,
                psy$Timepoint,
                p.adjust.method = 'bonf',
                paired=T)
```


## Graph psych measures
```{r}
psy <- read_q2metadata("data/aya_meta_costarica.tsv")
psy <- filter(psy, Consumed_Aya == "yes", Subject_Num !=17)

psy_long <- pivot_longer(psy,
                         cols = c(44, 47, 50, 53, 56, 59),
                         names_to = "Test",
                         values_to = "Score")
psy_long$Test <- as.factor(psy_long$Test)
psy_long$Test <- fct_recode(psy_long$Test,
                            "PCL5" = "PCL5_sum_1_2")
psy_long$Timepoint <- fct_recode(psy_long$Timepoint,
                                 "Before" = "1",
                                 "After" = "2")

p<- ggplot(psy_long, aes(x=Timepoint, y=Score, group=Subject_Num, color=Subject_Num))+
  geom_point()+
  geom_line()+
   theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    text = element_text(colour = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", 
                              size = 20, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.line = element_line(colour="black", size = 1),
    axis.ticks = element_line(colour="black", size = 1),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1)),
    axis.title.y = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 2, size=20),
    axis.title.x = element_text(vjust = -0.2, size = 20),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16),
    plot.background = element_blank(),
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text.x = element_text(size=16),
    legend.title=element_text(size=16),
    legend.text=element_text(size=16),
    legend.key=element_blank()) +
  guides(color = guide_legend(title="Subject"))+
  facet_wrap(~Test, scales="free")


#ggsave("figures/psychological_measures.png", width = 10, height = 8, units = "in")
```

### Individual graphs
```{r}
psy <- read_q2metadata("data/aya_meta_costarica.tsv")
psy <- filter(psy, Consumed_Aya == "yes", Subject_Num !=17)
psy$Timepoint <- fct_recode(psy$Timepoint,
                            "Before" = "1",
                            "After" = "2")
psy1 <- filter(psy, Timepoint == "Before")
psy2 <- filter(psy, Timepoint == "After")

# info on FFM/PCl-5
mean(psy1$Openness)
mean(psy2$Openness)
sd(psy1$Openness)
sd(psy2$Openness)

mean(psy1$Conscientiousness)
mean(psy2$Conscientiousness)
sd(psy1$Conscientiousness)
sd(psy2$Conscientiousness)

mean(psy1$Extraversion)
mean(psy2$Extraversion)
sd(psy1$Extraversion)
sd(psy2$Extraversion)

mean(psy1$Agreeableness)
mean(psy2$Agreeableness)
sd(psy1$Agreeableness)
sd(psy2$Agreeableness)

mean(psy1$Neuroticism)
mean(psy2$Neuroticism)
sd(psy1$Neuroticism)
sd(psy2$Neuroticism)

mean(psy1$PCL5_sum_1_2)
mean(psy2$PCL5_sum_1_2)
sd(psy1$PCL5_sum_1_2)
sd(psy2$PCL5_sum_1_2)

op <-ggplot(psy, aes(x=Timepoint, y=Openness, group=Subject_Num, color=Subject_Num))+
  geom_point()+
  geom_line()+
  # average line
  geom_segment(aes(x = .8, y = mean(psy1$Openness),
                   xend = 2.2, yend = mean(psy2$Openness)),
               color="black",
               size = 1,
               linetype =2) +
   # p = 0.02174
  geom_segment(aes(x = "Before", y = 4.8, xend = "After", yend = 4.8), color="black") +
  annotate(geom="text", x=1.5, y=4.9, label = "*", size = 8) +
  labs(title="Openness",
       y = "Score",
       x = "Timepoint")+
   theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    text = element_text(colour = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", 
                              size = 16, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.line = element_line(colour="black", size = 1),
    axis.ticks = element_line(colour="black", size = 1),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1)),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16),
    plot.background = element_blank(),
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text.x = element_text(size=16),
    legend.title=element_text(size=16),
    legend.text=element_text(size=16),
    legend.key=element_blank()) +
  guides(color = guide_legend(title="Participant"))+
  coord_cartesian(ylim=c(1.2,5.2))

cs <-ggplot(psy, aes(x=Timepoint, y=Conscientiousness, group=Subject_Num, color=Subject_Num))+
  geom_point()+
  geom_line()+
  geom_segment(aes(x = .8, y = mean(psy1$Conscientiousness),
                   xend = 2.2, yend = mean(psy2$Conscientiousness)),
               color="black",
               size = 1,
               linetype =2) +
  # p = 0.002516
  geom_segment(aes(x = "Before", y = 5, xend = "After", yend = 5), color="black") +
  annotate(geom="text", x=1.5, y=5.1, label = "**", size = 8) +
  labs(title="Conscientiousness",
       y = "Score",
       x = "Timepoint")+
   theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    text = element_text(colour = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", 
                              size = 16, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.line = element_line(colour="black", size = 1),
    axis.ticks = element_line(colour="black", size = 1),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1)),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16),
    plot.background = element_blank(),
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text.x = element_text(size=16),
    legend.title=element_text(size=16),
    legend.text=element_text(size=16),
    legend.key=element_blank()) +
  guides(color = guide_legend(title="Participant"))+
  coord_cartesian(ylim=c(1.2,5.2))

ex <- ggplot(psy, aes(x=Timepoint, y=Extraversion, group=Subject_Num, color=Subject_Num))+
  geom_point()+
  geom_line()+
  geom_segment(aes(x = .8, y = mean(psy1$Extraversion),
                   xend = 2.2, yend = mean(psy2$Extraversion)),
               color="black",
               size = 1,
               linetype =2) +
  # p = 0.0369
  geom_segment(aes(x = "Before", y = 5.05, xend = "After", yend = 5.05), color="black") +
  annotate(geom="text", x=1.5, y=5.15, label = "*", size = 8) +
  labs(title="Extraversion",
       y = "Score",
       x = "Timepoint")+
   theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    text = element_text(colour = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", 
                              size = 16, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.line = element_line(colour="black", size = 1),
    axis.ticks = element_line(colour="black", size = 1),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1)),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16),
    plot.background = element_blank(),
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text.x = element_text(size=16),
    legend.title=element_text(size=16),
    legend.text=element_text(size=16),
    legend.key=element_blank()) +
  guides(color = guide_legend(title="Participant"))+
  coord_cartesian(ylim=c(1.2,5.2))

ag <-ggplot(psy, aes(x=Timepoint, y=Agreeableness, group=Subject_Num, color=Subject_Num))+
  geom_point()+
  geom_line()+
  geom_segment(aes(x = .8, y = mean(psy1$Agreeableness),
                   xend = 2.2, yend = mean(psy2$Agreeableness)),
               color="black",
               size = 1,
               linetype =2) +
  # p = 0.00532 
  geom_segment(aes(x = "Before", y = 4.8, xend = "After", yend = 4.8), color="black") +
  annotate(geom="text", x=1.5, y=4.9, label = "**", size = 8) +
  labs(title="Agreeableness",
       y = "Score",
       x = "Timepoint")+
   theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    text = element_text(colour = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", 
                              size = 16, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.line = element_line(colour="black", size = 1),
    axis.ticks = element_line(colour="black", size = 1),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1)),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16),
    plot.background = element_blank(),
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text.x = element_text(size=16),
    legend.title=element_text(size=16),
    legend.text=element_text(size=16),
    legend.key=element_blank()) +
  guides(color = guide_legend(title="Participant"))+
  coord_cartesian(ylim=c(1.2,5.2))

ne <-ggplot(psy, aes(x=Timepoint, y=Neuroticism, group=Subject_Num, color=Subject_Num))+
  geom_point()+
  geom_line()+
  geom_segment(aes(x = .8, y = mean(psy1$Neuroticism),
                   xend = 2.2, yend = mean(psy2$Neuroticism)),
               color="black",
               size = 1,
               linetype =2) +
  # p = 0.002497
  geom_segment(aes(x = "Before", y = 4.2, xend = "After", yend = 4.2), color="black") +
  annotate(geom="text", x=1.5, y=4.3, label = "**", size = 8) +
  labs(title="Neuroticism",
       y = "Score",
       x = "Timepoint")+
   theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    text = element_text(colour = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", 
                              size = 16, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.line = element_line(colour="black", size = 1),
    axis.ticks = element_line(colour="black", size = 1),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1)),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16),
    plot.background = element_blank(),
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text.x = element_text(size=16),
    legend.title=element_text(size=16),
    legend.text=element_text(size=16),
    legend.key=element_blank()) +
  guides(color = guide_legend(title="Participant"))+
  coord_cartesian(ylim=c(1.2,5.2))

pl <- ggplot(psy, aes(x=Timepoint, y=PCL5_sum_1_2, group=Subject_Num, color=Subject_Num))+
  geom_point()+
  geom_line()+
  geom_segment(aes(x = .8, y = mean(psy1$PCL5_sum_1_2),
                   xend = 2.2, yend = mean(psy2$PCL5_sum_1_2)),
               color="black",
               size = 1,
               linetype =2) +
  # p = 0.02061
  geom_segment(aes(x = "Before", y = 72, xend = "After", yend = 72), color="black") +
  annotate(geom="text", x=1.5, y=73, label = "*", size = 8) +
  labs(title="PCL-5: PTSD Checklist",
       y = "Score",
       x = "Timepoint")+
   theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    text = element_text(colour = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", 
                              size = 16, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.line = element_line(colour="black", size = 1),
    axis.ticks = element_line(colour="black", size = 1),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1)),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16),
    plot.background = element_blank(),
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text.x = element_text(size=16),
    legend.title=element_text(size=16),
    legend.text=element_text(size=16),
    legend.key=element_blank()) +
  guides(color = guide_legend(title="Participant"))+
  coord_cartesian(ylim=c(5,75))

# grid of each plot
fig <- ggarrange(op, cs, ex, ag, ne, pl,
                 labels = c("A", "B", "C", "D", "E", "F"),
                 font.label = list(size = 18),
                 nrow = 2.5, ncol = 3,
                 common.legend = TRUE, legend="right")

annotate_figure(fig,
                left = text_grob("Score", 
                                 rot = 90, size = 16, face = "bold"),
                bottom = text_grob("Timepoint", size = 16, face = "bold"))

#ggsave(file="figures/psych_measures_panels.svg", width=14, height=8)


# only FFM
fig <- ggarrange(op, cs, ex, ag, ne,
                 labels = c("A", "B", "C", "D", "E"),
                 font.label = list(size = 18),
                 nrow = 2.5, ncol = 3,
                 common.legend = TRUE, legend="right")

annotate_figure(fig,
                left = text_grob("Score", 
                                 rot = 90, size = 16, face = "bold"),
                bottom = text_grob("Timepoint", size = 16, face = "bold"))

ggsave(file="figures/FFM_panels.svg", width=13, height=8)

pl +
   theme(axis.title.y = element_text(size = 16),
         axis.title.x = element_text(size = 16))

ggsave(file="figures/PCL5_panel.png", width=6, height=4)

```


## ASV analysis
Determine if change in species or genus correlates to change in score for FFM: neuroticism, extraversion, openness, agreeableness, conscientiousness.


```{r}
# get change in psychological scores
df <- read_q2metadata("data/aya_meta_costarica.tsv")
df <- filter(df, Consumed_Aya == "yes")

# FFM... or OCEAN
df$O_Change <- NA # openness
df$C_Change <- NA # conscientiousness
df$E_Change <- NA # extraversion
df$A_Change <- NA # agreeableness
df$N_Change <- NA # neuroticism

# PCL5 (PTSD score)
df$PCL_Change <- NA

psy_measures <- c("Openness",
                  "Conscientiousness",
                  "Extraversion",
                  "Agreeableness",
                  "Neuroticism",
                  "PCL5_sum_1_2")

evens <- seq(2,26, by = 2)
for (x in evens){
  for (psy in psy_measures){
    before <- df[x,psy]
    after <- df[x-1, psy]
    change <- after-before
    
    if (psy == "Openness"){
      df$O_Change[x-1] <- change
      df$O_Change[x] <- change
    }
    
    if (psy == "Conscientiousness"){
    df$C_Change[x-1] <- change
    df$C_Change[x] <- change
    }

    if (psy == "Extraversion"){
    df$E_Change[x-1] <- change
    df$E_Change[x] <- change
    }
    
    if (psy == "Agreeableness"){
    df$A_Change[x-1] <- change
    df$A_Change[x] <- change
    }
    
    if (psy == "Neuroticism"){
    df$N_Change[x-1] <- change
    df$N_Change[x] <- change
    }
    
    if (psy == "PCL5_sum_1_2"){
    df$PCL_Change[x-1] <- change
    df$PCL_Change[x] <- change
    }
  }
}

# pull out T1 so there aren't double entries
meta <- filter(df, Timepoint == 1)

# does change in relative abundance for each taxa predict the change in psychological measures?
# linear regression
rarefied_table <- read_qza("core-diversity-results-cr/rarefied_table.qza")$data

t_rare <- t(rarefied_table)
rare_df <- as.data.frame(t_rare)

# drop 0's
# drop low variance columns
i <- 0
for (col in 1:ncol(rare_df)){
  ifelse(sum(rare_df[,col]) == 0,
         rare_df[1:nrow(rare_df),col] <- NA,
                 ifelse(var(rare_df[,col]) < .05,
                            rare_df[1:nrow(rare_df),col] <- NA,
                            i <- i+1))
}
cols_dropped = ncol(rare_df) - i

rare_df_v <- rare_df[, colSums(is.na(rare_df)) == 0]

rare_change <- rare_df_v
rare_change[rare_change >= 0] <- NA

# calculate change in abundance between t1 and t2
for (col in 1:ncol(rare_change)){
  for (x in evens){
    before <- rare_df_v[,col][x-1]
    after <- rare_df_v[,col][x]
    tax_change <- after - before
    if (is.nan(tax_change) == TRUE){
      tax_change <- 0
    }
    if (is.infinite(tax_change) == TRUE){
      tax_change <- 0
    }
    rare_change[,col][x-1] <- tax_change
    rare_change[,col][x] <- tax_change
  }
}

# drop empties again
rare_change <- rare_change[, colSums(rare_change) != 0]

rare_change$Timepoint <- rep(c(1,2), 13)
# keep only t1 so not double entries
rare_change <- filter(rare_change, Timepoint == 1)

# drop subject 17 from both dataframes due to missing data
rare_change$ID <- rownames(rare_change)
rare_change$Subject <- substr(rare_change$ID, start = 1, stop = 2)
rare_change$Subject <- as.factor(rare_change$Subject)

meta_filt <- filter(meta, Subject_Num != "17")
rchange_filt <- filter(rare_change, Subject != "17")
# select() use will conflict with MASS package unless dplyr is specified
rchange_filt <- dplyr::select(rchange_filt, -Timepoint, -ID, -Subject)
taxonomy <- read_qza("data/taxonomy-silva.qza")$data %>% parse_taxonomy()
taxonomy_full <- read_qza("data/taxonomy-silva.qza")$data
rownames(taxonomy_full) <- taxonomy_full$Feature.ID


# dfs for p values of cor.test
cor_o <- rchange_filt[1,]
cor_c <- rchange_filt[1,]
cor_e <- rchange_filt[1,]
cor_a <- rchange_filt[1,]
cor_n <- rchange_filt[1,]
cor_p <- rchange_filt[1,]
psy_change <- meta_filt[,78:83]
rownames(psy_change) <- meta_filt$SampleID

# for each psych measure, and for each taxa, make df with stats results
# this has 5970 loops
fail_check <- 0
for (psy in 1:ncol(psy_change)){
  col_name <- colnames(psy_change[psy])
  for (tax in 1:ncol(rchange_filt)){
    p <- cor.test(psy_change[,psy], rchange_filt[,tax], method="pearson")$p.value
    ifelse(col_name == "O_Change",
           cor_o[,tax] <- p,
    ifelse(col_name == "C_Change",
           cor_c[,tax] <- p,
    ifelse(col_name == "E_Change",
           cor_e[,tax] <- p,
    ifelse(col_name == "A_Change",
           cor_a[,tax] <- p,
    ifelse(col_name == "N_Change",
           cor_n[,tax] <- p,
    ifelse(col_name == "PCL_Change",
           cor_p[,tax] <- p,
           fail_check + 1))))))
  }
}

# drop NAs caused by 0 standard deviation
cor_o <- cor_o[, colSums(is.na(cor_o)) == 0]
cor_c <- cor_c[, colSums(is.na(cor_c)) == 0]
cor_e <- cor_e[, colSums(is.na(cor_e)) == 0]
cor_a <- cor_a[, colSums(is.na(cor_a)) == 0]
cor_n <- cor_n[, colSums(is.na(cor_n)) == 0]
cor_p <- cor_p[, colSums(is.na(cor_p)) == 0]

# adjust for multiple comparisons? obliterates significance
q_o <- apply(cor_o, 2, function(x) p.adjust(x, method = "fdr", n = ncol(cor_o)))
q_c <- apply(cor_c, 2, function(x) p.adjust(x, method = "fdr", n = ncol(cor_c)))
q_e <- apply(cor_e, 2, function(x) p.adjust(x, method = "fdr", n = ncol(cor_e)))
q_a <- apply(cor_a, 2, function(x) p.adjust(x, method = "fdr", n = ncol(cor_a)))
q_n <- apply(cor_n, 2, function(x) p.adjust(x, method = "fdr", n = ncol(cor_n)))
q_p <- apply(cor_p, 2, function(x) p.adjust(x, method = "fdr", n = ncol(cor_p)))
qdf_o <- as.data.frame(q_o)
qdf_c <- as.data.frame(q_c)
qdf_e <- as.data.frame(q_e)
qdf_a <- as.data.frame(q_a)
qdf_n <- as.data.frame(q_n)
qdf_p <- as.data.frame(q_p)

# filter if p <.01
sig_o <- cor_o %>% keep(~ any(. <= .01))
sig_c <- cor_c %>% keep(~ any(. <= .01))
sig_e <- cor_e %>% keep(~ any(. <= .01))
sig_a <- cor_a %>% keep(~ any(. <= .01))
sig_n <- cor_n %>% keep(~ any(. <= .01))
sig_p <- cor_p %>% keep(~ any(. <= .01))
```

#### ASV Openness, Conscientiousness
```{r}
# check linearity
# Openness: looks linear
df_o <- rchange_filt[colnames(rchange_filt)[colnames(rchange_filt) %in% colnames(sig_o)]]
df_o <- cbind(O_Change = psy_change$O_Change, df_o)
# plot(df_o[,2], df_o$O_Change)
# m <- lm(df_o[,1] ~ df_o[,2])
# abline(m)
# legend("topleft",legend=paste("R2 is", format(summary(m)$r.squared,digits=3)))
# plot(m, which =2)
asvs <- colnames(df_o)
taxa <- taxonomy_full[asvs,"Taxon"]
# no species so will name based on genus
names_o <- give_nickname(taxa)

ggplot(df_o, aes(x = df_o[,1], y = df_o[,2])) +
  stat_poly_line() +
  stat_poly_eq(use_label(c("R2", "p")),
               hjust = -.5,
               size = 6) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(title = names_o[2],
       x = "Change in Openness",
       y = "Change in abundance") +
    theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    text = element_text(colour = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "italic", 
                              size = 17, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.line = element_line(colour="black", size = 1),
    axis.ticks = element_line(colour="black", size = 1),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1)),
    axis.title.y = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 2, size=18),
    axis.title.x = element_text(vjust = -0.2, size = 18),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16),
    plot.background = element_blank(),
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text.x = element_text(size=16),
    legend.title=element_text(size=16),
    legend.text=element_text(size=16),
    legend.key=element_blank())
#ggsave("figures/openness_cor1.png", width = 8, height = 6, units = "in")

# Conscientiousness: both non-linear
df_c <- rchange_filt[colnames(rchange_filt)[colnames(rchange_filt) %in% colnames(sig_c)]]
df_c <- cbind(E_Change = psy_change$C_Change, df_c)

# plots
plot(df_c[,3], df_o$C_Change)
m <- lm(df_c[,1] ~ df_c[,3])
abline(m)
legend("topleft",legend=paste("R2 is", format(summary(m)$r.squared,digits=3)))
plot(m, which =2)
```

#### ASV Extraversion Panels
Code help from  https://www.statology.org/dffits-in-r/
```{r}
# Extraversion: some linear
# okay... rows that have only 1 non-zero are not going to be linear
# so let's filter those out
df_e <- rchange_filt[colnames(rchange_filt)[colnames(rchange_filt) %in% colnames(sig_e)]]
cols_to_drop <- names(df_e)[apply(df_e != 0, 2, sum) == 1]
df_e <- df_e[, !(names(df_e) %in% cols_to_drop)]
df_e <- cbind(E_Change = psy_change$E_Change, df_e)


# Check outliers
# columns 3, 8, 9, 11, 12, 14, 17, 19 look linear
m <- lm(df_e$E_Change ~ df_e[,3], data=df_e)
dffits(m)
#find number of predictors in model
p <- length(m$coefficients)-1
#find number of observations
n <- nrow(df_e)
#calculate DFFITS threshold value
thresh <- 2*sqrt(p/n)
#plot DFFITS values for each observation
plot(dffits(m), type = 'h')
abline(h = thresh, lty = 2)
abline(h = -thresh, lty = 2)


# plot(df_e[,2], df_e$E_Change)
# m <- lm(df_e[,1] ~ df_e[,2])
# abline(m)

asvs <- colnames(df_e)
taxa <- taxonomy_full[asvs,"Taxon"]
# if not species, then genus
names_e <- give_nickname(taxa, one_name = T)


# columns 3, 8, 9, 11, 12, 14, 17, 19 look linear
e3 <- ggplot(df_e, aes(x = df_e[,1], y = df_e[,3])) +
  stat_poly_line() +
  stat_poly_eq(use_label(c("R2", "p")),
               size = 6) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(title = names_e[3]) +
    theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    text = element_text(colour = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "italic", 
                              size = 17, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.line = element_line(colour="black", size = 1),
    axis.ticks = element_line(colour="black", size = 1),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1)),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16),
    plot.background = element_blank(),
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text.x = element_text(size=16),
    legend.title=element_text(size=16),
    legend.text=element_text(size=16),
    legend.key=element_blank())

# columns 3, 8, 9, 11, 12, 14, 17, 19 look linear
e8 <- ggplot(df_e, aes(x = df_e[,1], y = df_e[,8])) +
  stat_poly_line() +
  stat_poly_eq(use_label(c("R2", "p")),
               size = 6) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(title = names_e[8]) +
    theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    text = element_text(colour = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "italic", 
                              size = 17, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.line = element_line(colour="black", size = 1),
    axis.ticks = element_line(colour="black", size = 1),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1)),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16),
    plot.background = element_blank(),
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text.x = element_text(size=16),
    legend.title=element_text(size=16),
    legend.text=element_text(size=16),
    legend.key=element_blank())

# columns 3, 8, 9, 11, 12, 14, 17, 19 look linear
e9 <- ggplot(df_e, aes(x = df_e[,1], y = df_e[,9])) +
  stat_poly_line() +
  stat_poly_eq(use_label(c("R2", "p")),
               size = 6) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(title = names_e[9]) +
    theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    text = element_text(colour = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "italic", 
                              size = 17, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.line = element_line(colour="black", size = 1),
    axis.ticks = element_line(colour="black", size = 1),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1)),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16),
    plot.background = element_blank(),
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text.x = element_text(size=16),
    legend.title=element_text(size=16),
    legend.text=element_text(size=16),
    legend.key=element_blank())

# columns 3, 8, 9, 11, 12, 14, 17, 19 look linear
e11 <- ggplot(df_e, aes(x = df_e[,1], y = df_e[,11])) +
  stat_poly_line() +
  stat_poly_eq(use_label(c("R2", "p")),
               size = 6) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(title = names_e[11]) +
    theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    text = element_text(colour = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "italic", 
                              size = 17, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.line = element_line(colour="black", size = 1),
    axis.ticks = element_line(colour="black", size = 1),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1)),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16),
    plot.background = element_blank(),
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text.x = element_text(size=16),
    legend.title=element_text(size=16),
    legend.text=element_text(size=16),
    legend.key=element_blank())

# columns 3, 8, 9, 11, 12, 14, 17, 19 look linear
e12 <- ggplot(df_e, aes(x = df_e[,1], y = df_e[,12])) +
  stat_poly_line() +
  stat_poly_eq(use_label(c("R2", "p")),
               size = 6) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(title = names_e[12]) +
    theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    text = element_text(colour = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "italic", 
                              size = 17, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.line = element_line(colour="black", size = 1),
    axis.ticks = element_line(colour="black", size = 1),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1)),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16),
    plot.background = element_blank(),
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text.x = element_text(size=16),
    legend.title=element_text(size=16),
    legend.text=element_text(size=16),
    legend.key=element_blank())

# columns 3, 8, 9, 11, 12, 14, 17, 19 look linear
e14 <- ggplot(df_e, aes(x = df_e[,1], y = df_e[,14])) +
  stat_poly_line() +
  stat_poly_eq(use_label(c("R2", "p")),
               size = 6) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(title = names_e[14]) +
    theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    text = element_text(colour = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "italic", 
                              size = 17, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.line = element_line(colour="black", size = 1),
    axis.ticks = element_line(colour="black", size = 1),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1)),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16),
    plot.background = element_blank(),
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text.x = element_text(size=16),
    legend.title=element_text(size=16),
    legend.text=element_text(size=16),
    legend.key=element_blank())

# columns 3, 8, 9, 11, 12, 14, 17, 19 look linear
e17 <- ggplot(df_e, aes(x = df_e[,1], y = df_e[,17])) +
  stat_poly_line() +
  stat_poly_eq(use_label(c("R2", "p")),
               size = 6) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(title = names_e[17]) +
    theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    text = element_text(colour = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "italic", 
                              size = 17, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.line = element_line(colour="black", size = 1),
    axis.ticks = element_line(colour="black", size = 1),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1)),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16),
    plot.background = element_blank(),
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text.x = element_text(size=16),
    legend.title=element_text(size=16),
    legend.text=element_text(size=16),
    legend.key=element_blank())

# columns 3, 8, 9, 11, 12, 14, 17, 19 look linear
e19 <- ggplot(df_e, aes(x = df_e[,1], y = df_e[,19])) +
  stat_poly_line() +
  stat_poly_eq(use_label(c("R2", "p")),
               size = 6) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(title = names_e[19]) +
    theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    text = element_text(colour = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "italic", 
                              size = 17, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.line = element_line(colour="black", size = 1),
    axis.ticks = element_line(colour="black", size = 1),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1)),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16),
    plot.background = element_blank(),
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text.x = element_text(size=16),
    legend.title=element_text(size=16),
    legend.text=element_text(size=16),
    legend.key=element_blank())

# grid of each plot
fig <- ggarrange(e3, e8, e9, e11, e12, e14, e17, e19,
                 labels = c("A", "B", "C", "D", "E", "F", "G", "H"),
                 nrow = 2, ncol = 4)

annotate_figure(fig,
                left = text_grob("Change in abundance", 
                                 rot = 90, size = 20, face = "bold"),
                bottom = text_grob("Change in Extraversion", size = 20, face = "bold"))


#ggsave(file="figures/extraversion_ASV_cor.png", width=16, height=8)

```

#### ASV: Other
```{r}
# Agreeableness: not linear
df_a <- rchange_filt[colnames(rchange_filt)[colnames(rchange_filt) %in% colnames(sig_a)]]
df_a <- cbind(A_Change = psy_change$A_Change, df_a)


# Neuroticism: one linear
df_n <- rchange_filt[colnames(rchange_filt)[colnames(rchange_filt) %in% colnames(sig_n)]]
df_n <- cbind(N_Change = psy_change$N_Change, df_n)

asvs <- colnames(df_n)
taxa <- taxonomy_full[asvs,"Taxon"]
# if not species, then genus
names_n <- give_nickname(taxa, one_name = T)

# Neuroticism plot
ggplot(df_n, aes(x = df_n[,1], y = df_n[,3])) +
  stat_poly_line() +
  stat_poly_eq(use_label(c("R2", "p")),
               hjust = -.5,
               size = 6) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(title = names_n[3],
       x = "Change in Neuroticism",
       y = "Change in abundance") +
    theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    text = element_text(colour = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "italic", 
                              size = 20, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.line = element_line(colour="black", size = 1),
    axis.ticks = element_line(colour="black", size = 1),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1)),
    axis.title.y = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 2, size=18),
    axis.title.x = element_text(vjust = -0.2, size = 18),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16),
    plot.background = element_blank(),
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text.x = element_text(size=16),
    legend.title=element_text(size=16),
    legend.text=element_text(size=16),
    legend.key=element_blank())

#ggsave("figures/neuro_cor3.png", width = 8, height = 6, units = "in")

# No PCL-5 changes
```


## Genus analysis
Code help from  https://www.statology.org/dffits-in-r/
https://online.stat.psu.edu/stat462/node/173/
"In the end, the analyst should analyze the data set twice — once with and once without the flagged data points. If the data points significantly alter the outcome of the regression analysis, then the researcher should report the results of both analyses."
```{r}
taxonomy <- read_qza("data/taxonomy-silva.qza")$data %>% parse_taxonomy()
taxonomy_full <- read_qza("data/taxonomy-silva.qza")$data
rownames(taxonomy_full) <- taxonomy_full$Feature.ID

# copy-pasted from above
# get change in psychological scores
df <- read_q2metadata("data/aya_meta_costarica.tsv")
df <- filter(df, Consumed_Aya == "yes")

# FFM (OCEAN)
df$O_Change <- NA # openness
df$C_Change <- NA # conscientiousness
df$E_Change <- NA # extraversion
df$A_Change <- NA # agreeableness
df$N_Change <- NA # neuroticism

# PCL5 (PTSD score)
df$PCL_Change <- NA

psy_measures <- c("Openness",
                  "Conscientiousness",
                  "Extraversion",
                  "Agreeableness",
                  "Neuroticism",
                  "PCL5_sum_1_2")

evens <- seq(2,26, by = 2)
for (x in evens){
  for (psy in psy_measures){
    before <- df[x,psy]
    after <- df[x-1, psy]
    change <- after-before
    
    if (psy == "Openness"){
      df$O_Change[x-1] <- change
      df$O_Change[x] <- change
    }
    
    if (psy == "Conscientiousness"){
    df$C_Change[x-1] <- change
    df$C_Change[x] <- change
    }

    if (psy == "Extraversion"){
    df$E_Change[x-1] <- change
    df$E_Change[x] <- change
    }
    
    if (psy == "Agreeableness"){
    df$A_Change[x-1] <- change
    df$A_Change[x] <- change
    }
    
    if (psy == "Neuroticism"){
    df$N_Change[x-1] <- change
    df$N_Change[x] <- change
    }
    
    if (psy == "PCL5_sum_1_2"){
    df$PCL_Change[x-1] <- change
    df$PCL_Change[x] <- change
    }
  }
}

# pull out T1 so there aren't double entries
meta <- dplyr::filter(df, Timepoint == 1)


# GENUS linear regression
rarefied_table <- read_qza("core-diversity-results-cr/rarefied_table.qza")$data
r_df <- as.data.frame(rarefied_table)
taxonomy <- read_qza("data/taxonomy-silva.qza")$data %>% parse_taxonomy()

# using the ASV rownames, match genera to ASVs
r_df$Genus <- taxonomy[match(row.names(r_df), row.names(taxonomy)),"Genus"]
r_df$Genus <- as.factor(r_df$Genus)

# add rows based on column
r_df <- ddply(r_df, "Genus", numcolwise(sum))
r_df <- dplyr::filter(r_df, is.na(Genus) == FALSE)

# clean up
rdf <- dplyr::select(r_df, -Genus)
rdf <- t(rdf)
rdf <- as.data.frame(rdf)
colnames(rdf) <- r_df$Genus

# drop low variance columns
zeros_count <- colSums(rdf == 0)
to_drop <- names(zeros_count[zeros_count > 24])
rdf2 <- rdf[, !(names(rdf)) %in% to_drop]

# copy-pasted from above
rare_change <- rdf2
rare_change[rare_change >= 0] <- NA

# calculate change in abundance between t1 and t2
for (col in 1:ncol(rare_change)){
  for (x in evens){
    tax_change <- rdf2[,col][x] - rdf2[,col][x-1]
    rare_change[,col][x-1] <- tax_change
    rare_change[,col][x] <- tax_change
  }
}

rare_change$Timepoint <- rep(c(1,2), 13)
# keep only t1 so not double entries
rare_change <- dplyr::filter(rare_change, Timepoint == 1)

# drop subject 17 from both dataframes due to missing data
rare_change$ID <- rownames(rare_change)
rare_change$Subject <- substr(rare_change$ID, start = 1, stop = 2)
rare_change$Subject <- as.factor(rare_change$Subject)
rownames(rare_change) <- rare_change$Subject
  
meta_filt <- filter(meta, Subject_Num != "17")
rchange_filt <- filter(rare_change, Subject != "17")
# select() use will conflict with MASS package unless dplyr is specified
rchange_filt <- dplyr::select(rchange_filt, -Timepoint, -ID, -Subject)

# arbitrary dfs for p values of cor.test
cor_o <- rchange_filt[1,]
cor_c <- rchange_filt[1,]
cor_e <- rchange_filt[1,]
cor_a <- rchange_filt[1,]
cor_n <- rchange_filt[1,]
cor_p <- rchange_filt[1,]
psy_change <- meta_filt[,78:83]
rownames(psy_change) <- meta_filt$Subject_Num

# for each psych measure, and for each taxa, make df with stats results
# this has 5970 loops
fail_check <- 0
for (psy in 1:ncol(psy_change)){
  col_name <- colnames(psy_change[psy])
  for (tax in 1:ncol(rchange_filt)){
    p <- cor.test(psy_change[,psy], rchange_filt[,tax], method="pearson")$p.value
    ifelse(col_name == "O_Change",
           cor_o[,tax] <- p,
    ifelse(col_name == "C_Change",
           cor_c[,tax] <- p,
    ifelse(col_name == "E_Change",
           cor_e[,tax] <- p,
    ifelse(col_name == "A_Change",
           cor_a[,tax] <- p,
    ifelse(col_name == "N_Change",
           cor_n[,tax] <- p,
    ifelse(col_name == "PCL_Change",
           cor_p[,tax] <- p,
           fail_check + 1))))))
  }
}

# drop NAs caused by 0 standard deviation
cor_o <- cor_o[, colSums(is.na(cor_o)) == 0]
cor_c <- cor_c[, colSums(is.na(cor_c)) == 0]
cor_e <- cor_e[, colSums(is.na(cor_e)) == 0]
cor_a <- cor_a[, colSums(is.na(cor_a)) == 0]
cor_n <- cor_n[, colSums(is.na(cor_n)) == 0]
cor_p <- cor_p[, colSums(is.na(cor_p)) == 0]

# adjust for multiple comparisons? obliterates significance
q_o <- apply(cor_o, 2, function(x) p.adjust(x, method = "fdr", n = ncol(cor_o)))
q_c <- apply(cor_c, 2, function(x) p.adjust(x, method = "fdr", n = ncol(cor_c)))
q_e <- apply(cor_e, 2, function(x) p.adjust(x, method = "fdr", n = ncol(cor_e)))
q_a <- apply(cor_a, 2, function(x) p.adjust(x, method = "fdr", n = ncol(cor_a)))
q_n <- apply(cor_n, 2, function(x) p.adjust(x, method = "fdr", n = ncol(cor_n)))
q_p <- apply(cor_p, 2, function(x) p.adjust(x, method = "fdr", n = ncol(cor_p)))
qdf_o <- as.data.frame(q_o)
qdf_c <- as.data.frame(q_c)
qdf_e <- as.data.frame(q_e)
qdf_a <- as.data.frame(q_a)
qdf_n <- as.data.frame(q_n)
qdf_p <- as.data.frame(q_p)

# filter if p <.01
sig_o <- cor_o %>% keep(~ any(. <= .01))
sig_c <- cor_c %>% keep(~ any(. <= .01))
sig_e <- cor_e %>% keep(~ any(. <= .01))
sig_a <- cor_a %>% keep(~ any(. <= .01))
sig_n <- cor_n %>% keep(~ any(. <= .01))
sig_p <- cor_p %>% keep(~ any(. <= .01))
```

#### Genus: Openness
```{r} 
# check linearity
# Openness: has one, looks linear
df_o <- rchange_filt[colnames(rchange_filt)[colnames(rchange_filt) %in% colnames(sig_o)]]
df_o <- cbind(O_Change = psy_change$O_Change, df_o)

# Check influence using DFFITS
m <- lm(df_o[,1] ~ df_o[,2], data=df_o)
summary(m)
dffits(m)
df_fits <- as.data.frame(dffits(m))
colnames(df_fits) <- "Influence"
# find number of predictors in model
p <- length(m$coefficients)-1
# find number of observations
n <- nrow(df_o)
#  calculate DFFITS threshold value
thresh <- 2*sqrt(p/n)
#plot DFFITS values for each observation
plot(dffits(m), type = 'h')
abline(h = thresh, lty = 2)
abline(h = -thresh, lty = 2)
# make df with influence
o_inf <- cbind(df_fits,
               "N_Change" = df_o$O_Change,
               "Agathobacter" = df_o$Agathobacter)
o_inf$Influence <- abs(o_inf$Influence)

# Drop highest influence
o_drop <- o_inf[-which(o_inf$Influence == max(o_inf$Influence)),]

m2 <- lm(o_drop[,2] ~ o_drop[,3], data=o_drop)
summary(m2)

# Custom legend stuff
min_inf <- round(min(o_inf$Influence),2)
max_inf <- round(max(o_inf$Influence),2)
mid_inf <- round((max_inf-min_inf)/2,2)

min_drop <- round(min(o_drop$Influence),2)
max_drop <- round(max(o_drop$Influence),2)
mid_drop <- round((max_drop-min_drop)/2, 2)


ggplot(o_inf, aes(x = o_inf[,3], y = o_inf[,2], color = Influence,
                  label=rownames(o_inf))) +
  stat_poly_line(color="darkgrey", size = 1) +
  stat_poly_eq(use_label(c("R2")),
               hjust = -.5,
               size = 6) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "black", high = "orange",
                       breaks = c(min_inf, mid_inf, max_inf),
                       labels = c(min_inf, mid_inf, max_inf)) +   
  geom_text_repel(size=6) +
  labs(title = names(df_o)[2],
       x = "Change in abundance",
       y = "Change in Openness") +
    theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    text = element_text(color = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 20, hjust = 0.5,
                              face = "italic"),
    axis.line = element_line(color="black", size = 1),
    axis.ticks = element_line(color="black", size = 1),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1)),
    axis.title.y = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 2, size=18),
    axis.title.x = element_text(vjust = -0.2, size = 18),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16),
    plot.background = element_blank(),
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text.x = element_text(size=16),
    legend.title=element_text(size=16),
    legend.text=element_text(size=16),
    legend.key=element_blank())
#ggsave("figures/openness_genus1.png", width = 10, height = 8, units = "in")


o1 <- ggplot(o_inf, aes(x = o_inf[,2], y = o_inf[,3], color = Influence,
                  label=rownames(o_inf))) +
  stat_poly_line(color="darkgrey", size = 1) +
  stat_poly_eq(use_label(c("R2")),
               hjust = -.5,
               size = 6) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "black", high = "orange",
                       breaks = c(min_inf, mid_inf, max_inf),
                       labels = c(min_inf, mid_inf, max_inf)) +   
  geom_text_repel(size=6) +
  labs(title = "All samples") +
    theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    text = element_text(color = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 20, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.line = element_line(color="black", size = 1),
    axis.ticks = element_line(color="black", size = 1),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1)),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16),
    plot.background = element_blank(),
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text.x = element_text(size=16),
    legend.title=element_text(size=16),
    legend.text=element_text(size=16),
    legend.key=element_blank())
    
o2 <- ggplot(o_drop, aes(x = o_drop[,2], y = o_drop[,3], color = Influence,
                   label = rownames(o_drop))) +
  stat_poly_line(color="darkgrey", size = 1) +
  stat_poly_eq(use_label(c("R2", "p")),
               hjust = -.5,
               size = 6) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "black", high = "orange",
                       breaks = c(min_drop, mid_drop, max_drop),
                       labels = c(min_drop, mid_drop, max_drop)) +
  geom_text_repel(size=6) +
  labs(title = "Dropped highest influence point") +
    theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    text = element_text(color = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 20, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.line = element_line(color="black", size = 1),
    axis.ticks = element_line(color="black", size = 1),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1)),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16),
    plot.background = element_blank(),
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text.x = element_text(size=16),
    legend.title=element_text(size=16),
    legend.text=element_text(size=16),
    legend.key=element_blank())


fig <- ggarrange(o1, o2,
                 labels = c("A", "B"),
                 nrow = 1, ncol = 2)

annotate_figure(fig,
                top = text_grob(names(df_o[2]),
                                size = 22, face = "bold.italic"),
                left = text_grob("Change in abundance",
                                 rot = 90, size = 20, face = "bold"),
                bottom = text_grob("Change in Openness", size = 20, face = "bold"))

#ggsave("figures/penness_genus_influence.png", width = 14, height = 7, units = "in")

```

#### Genus: Conscientiousness
```{r}
# Conscientiousness
df_c <- rchange_filt[colnames(rchange_filt)[colnames(rchange_filt) %in% colnames(sig_c)]]
df_c <- cbind(C_Change = psy_change$C_Change, df_c)

weird_name <- filter(taxonomy,
                     Genus == names(df_c)[3])
fixed_name <- paste(weird_name$Family[1], names(df_c)[3])
df_c <- df_c[,-2]
colnames(df_c) <- c("C_Change", fixed_name)

# Check outliers
m <- lm(df_c[,1] ~ df_c[,2], data=df_c)
summary(m)
dffits(m)
df_fits <- as.data.frame(dffits(m))
colnames(df_fits) <- "Influence"
#find number of predictors in model
p <- length(m$coefficients)-1
#find number of observations
n <- nrow(df_c)
#calculate DFFITS threshold value
thresh <- 2*sqrt(p/n)
#plot DFFITS values for each observation
plot(dffits(m), type = 'h')
abline(h = thresh, lty = 2)
abline(h = -thresh, lty = 2)
# make df with influence
c_inf <- cbind(df_fits,
               "C_Change" = df_c$C_Change,
               fixed_name = df_c$`Ruminococcaceae UBA1819`)

c_inf$Influence <- abs(c_inf$Influence)

# Drop highest influence
c_drop <- c_inf[-which(c_inf$Influence == max(c_inf$Influence)),]

# Custom legend stuff
min_inf <- round(min(c_inf$Influence),2)
max_inf <- round(max(c_inf$Influence),2)
mid_inf <- round((max_inf-min_inf)/2,2)

min_drop <- round(min(c_drop$Influence),2)
max_drop <- round(max(c_drop$Influence),2)
mid_drop <- round((max_drop-min_drop)/2, 2)


# no longer significant or sloped after removal of high influence outlier
ggplot(c_inf, aes(x = c_inf[,3], y = c_inf[,2], color = Influence,
                  label=rownames(c_inf))) +
  stat_poly_line(color="darkgrey", size = 1) +
  stat_poly_eq(use_label(c("R2", "p")),
               hjust = -.5,
               size = 6) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "black", high = "orange",
                       breaks = c(min_inf, mid_inf, .91),
                       labels = c(min_inf, mid_inf, .91)) +   
  geom_text_repel(size=6) +
  labs(title = names(df_c)[2],
       x = "Change in abundance",
       y = "Change in Conscientiousness") +
    theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    text = element_text(color = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 20, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.line = element_line(color="black", size = 1),
    axis.ticks = element_line(color="black", size = 1),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1)),
    axis.title.y = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 2, size=18),
    axis.title.x = element_text(vjust = -0.2, size = 18),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16),
    plot.background = element_blank(),
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text.x = element_text(size=16),
    legend.title=element_text(size=16),
    legend.text=element_text(size=16),
    legend.key=element_blank())
#ggsave("figures/consc_genus_allsamples.png", width = 10, height = 8, units = "in")


c1 <- ggplot(c_inf, aes(x = c_inf[,2], y = c_inf[,3], color = Influence,
                  label=rownames(c_inf))) +
  stat_poly_line(color="darkgrey", size = 1) +
  stat_poly_eq(use_label(c("R2", "p")),
               hjust = -.5,
               size = 6) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "black", high = "orange",
                       breaks = c(min_inf, mid_inf, .91),
                       labels = c(min_inf, mid_inf, .91)) +   
  geom_text_repel(size=6) +
  labs(title = "All samples") +
    theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    text = element_text(color = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 20, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.line = element_line(color="black", size = 1),
    axis.ticks = element_line(color="black", size = 1),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1)),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16),
    plot.background = element_blank(),
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text.x = element_text(size=16),
    legend.title=element_text(size=16),
    legend.text=element_text(size=16),
    legend.key=element_blank())
    
c2 <- ggplot(c_drop, aes(x = c_drop[,2], y = c_drop[,3], color = Influence,
                   label = rownames(c_drop))) +
  stat_poly_line(color="darkgrey", size = 1) +
  stat_poly_eq(use_label(c("R2", "p")),
               hjust = -.5,
               size = 6) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "black", high = "orange",
                       breaks = c(min_drop, mid_drop, max_drop),
                       labels = c(min_drop, mid_drop, max_drop)) +
  geom_text_repel(size=6) +
  labs(title = "Dropped highest influence point") +
    theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    text = element_text(color = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 20, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.line = element_line(color="black", size = 1),
    axis.ticks = element_line(color="black", size = 1),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1)),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16),
```
