```{r}
    plot.background = element_blank(),
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text.x = element_text(size=16),
    legend.title=element_text(size=16),
    legend.text=element_text(size=16),
    legend.key=element_blank())


fig <- ggarrange(c1, c2,
                 labels = c("A", "B"),
                 nrow = 1, ncol = 2)

annotate_figure(fig,
                top = text_grob(names(df_c[2]),
                                size = 22, face = "bold"),
                left = text_grob("Change in abundance",
                                 rot = 90,
                                 size = 20,
                                 face = "bold"),
                bottom = text_grob("Change in Conscientiousness",
                                   size = 20, face = "bold"))

#ggsave("figures/consc_genus_influence.png", width = 14, height = 7, units = "in")

```


#### Genus: Extraversion1
```{r}
# Extraversion: some linear
# okay... rows that have only 1 non-zero are not going to be linear
# so let's filter those out
df_e <- rchange_filt[colnames(rchange_filt)[colnames(rchange_filt) %in% colnames(sig_e)]]
cols_to_drop <- names(df_e)[apply(df_e != 0, 2, sum) == 1]
df_e <- df_e[, !(names(df_e) %in% cols_to_drop)]
df_e <- cbind(E_Change = psy_change$E_Change, df_e)


# Check outliers
# column 2
m <- lm(df_e[,1] ~ df_e[,2], data=df_e)
summary(m)
dffits(m)
df_fits <- as.data.frame(dffits(m))
colnames(df_fits) <- "Influence"
#find number of predictors in model
p <- length(m$coefficients)-1
#find number of observations
n <- nrow(df_e)
#calculate DFFITS threshold value
thresh <- 2*sqrt(p/n)
#plot DFFITS values for each observation
plot(dffits(m), type = 'h')
abline(h = thresh, lty = 2)
abline(h = -thresh, lty = 2)
# make df with influence
e_inf <- cbind(df_fits, "E_Change" = df_e$E_Change,
               "[Eubacterium]_coprostanoligenes_group" = df_e$`[Eubacterium]_coprostanoligenes_group`)
e_inf$Influence <- abs(e_inf$Influence)

# Drop highest influence
e_drop <- e_inf[-which(e_inf$Influence == max(e_inf$Influence)),]

# Custom legend stuff
min_inf <- round(min(e_inf$Influence),2)
max_inf <- round(max(e_inf$Influence),2)
mid_inf <- round((max_inf-min_inf)/2,2)

min_drop <- round(min(e_drop$Influence),2)
max_drop <- round(max(e_drop$Influence),2)
mid_drop <- round((max_drop-min_drop)/2, 2)

ggplot(e_inf, aes(x = e_inf[,2], y = e_inf[,3], color = Influence,
                  label=rownames(e_inf))) +
  stat_poly_line(color="darkgrey", size = 1) +
  stat_poly_eq(use_label(c("R2", "p")),
               hjust = -.5,
               size = 6) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "black", high = "orange",
                       breaks = c(.1, mid_inf, max_inf),
                       labels = c(.1, mid_inf, max_inf)) +   
  geom_text_repel(size=6) +
  labs(title = names(df_e)[2],
       x = "Change in Extraversion",
       y = "Change in abundance") +
    theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    text = element_text(color = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 20, hjust = 0.5,
                              face="italic"),
    axis.line = element_line(color="black", size = 1),
    axis.ticks = element_line(color="black", size = 1),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1)),
    axis.title.y = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 2, size=18),
    axis.title.x = element_text(vjust = -0.2, size = 18),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16),
    plot.background = element_blank(),
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text.x = element_text(size=16),
    legend.title=element_text(size=16),
    legend.text=element_text(size=16),
    legend.key=element_blank())
#ggsave("figures/extra1_genus_allsamples.png", width = 10, height = 8, units = "in")


e1 <- ggplot(e_inf, aes(x = e_inf[,2], y = e_inf[,3], color = Influence,
                  label=rownames(e_inf))) +
  stat_poly_line(color="darkgrey", size = 1) +
  stat_poly_eq(use_label(c("R2", "p")),
               hjust = -.5,
               size = 6) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "black", high = "orange",
                       breaks = c(.1, mid_inf, max_inf),
                       labels = c(.1, mid_inf, max_inf)) +   
  geom_text_repel(size=6) +
  labs(title = "All samples") +
    theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    text = element_text(color = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 20, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.line = element_line(color="black", size = 1),
    axis.ticks = element_line(color="black", size = 1),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1)),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16),
    plot.background = element_blank(),
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text.x = element_text(size=16),
    legend.title=element_text(size=16),
    legend.text=element_text(size=16),
    legend.key=element_blank())
    
e2 <- ggplot(e_drop, aes(x = e_drop[,2], y = e_drop[,3], color = Influence,
                   label = rownames(e_drop))) +
  stat_poly_line(color="darkgrey", size = 1) +
  stat_poly_eq(use_label(c("R2", "p")),
               hjust = -.5,
               size = 6) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "black", high = "orange",
                       breaks = c(.01, mid_drop, .72),
                       labels = c(.01, mid_drop, .72)) +
  geom_text_repel(size=6) +
  labs(title = "Dropped highest influence point") +
    theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    text = element_text(color = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 20, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.line = element_line(color="black", size = 1),
    axis.ticks = element_line(color="black", size = 1),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1)),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16),
    plot.background = element_blank(),
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text.x = element_text(size=16),
    legend.title=element_text(size=16),
    legend.text=element_text(size=16),
    legend.key=element_blank())


fig <- ggarrange(e1, e2,
                 labels = c("A", "B"),
                 nrow = 1, ncol = 2)

annotate_figure(fig,
                top = text_grob(names(df_e[2]),
                                size = 22, face = "bold.italic"),
                left = text_grob("Change in abundance",
                                 rot = 90,
                                 size = 20,
                                 face = "bold"),
                bottom = text_grob("Change in Extraversion",
                                   size = 20, face = "bold"))

#ggsave("figures/extra1_genus_influence.png", width = 14, height = 7, units = "in")
```


##### Genus: Extraversion2
Run Extraversion 1 first
```{r}

# Check outliers
# column 3
m <- lm(df_e[,1] ~ df_e[,3], data=df_e)
summary(m)
dffits(m)
df_fits <- as.data.frame(dffits(m))
colnames(df_fits) <- "Influence"
#find number of predictors in model
p <- length(m$coefficients)-1
#find number of observations
n <- nrow(df_e)
#calculate DFFITS threshold value
thresh <- 2*sqrt(p/n)
#plot DFFITS values for each observation
plot(dffits(m), type = 'h')
abline(h = thresh, lty = 2)
abline(h = -thresh, lty = 2)
# make df with influence
e_inf <- cbind(df_fits, "E_Change" = df_e$E_Change,
               "Christensenellaceae_R-7_group" = df_e$`Christensenellaceae_R-7_group`)
e_inf$Influence <- abs(e_inf$Influence)

# Drop highest influence
e_drop <- e_inf[-which(e_inf$Influence == max(e_inf$Influence)),]

# Custom legend stuff
min_inf <- round(min(e_inf$Influence),2)
max_inf <- round(max(e_inf$Influence),2)
mid_inf <- round((max_inf-min_inf)/2,2)

min_drop <- round(min(e_drop$Influence),2)
max_drop <- round(max(e_drop$Influence),2)
mid_drop <- round((max_drop-min_drop)/2, 2)

ggplot(e_inf, aes(x = e_inf[,2], y = e_inf[,3], color = Influence,
                  label=rownames(e_inf))) +
  stat_poly_line(color="darkgrey", size = 1) +
  stat_poly_eq(use_label(c("R2", "p")),
               hjust = -.5,
               size = 6) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "black", high = "orange",
                       breaks = c(min_inf, mid_inf, max_inf),
                       labels = c(min_inf, mid_inf, max_inf)) +   
  geom_text_repel(size=6) +
  labs(title = names(df_e)[3],
       x = "Change in Extraversion",
       y = "Change in abundance") +
    theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    text = element_text(color = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 20, hjust = 0.5,
                              face="italic"),
    axis.line = element_line(color="black", size = 1),
    axis.ticks = element_line(color="black", size = 1),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1)),
    axis.title.y = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 2, size=18),
    axis.title.x = element_text(vjust = -0.2, size = 18),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16),
    plot.background = element_blank(),
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text.x = element_text(size=16),
    legend.title=element_text(size=16),
    legend.text=element_text(size=16),
    legend.key=element_blank())
#ggsave("figures/extra2_genus_allsamples.png", width = 10, height = 8, units = "in")


e1 <- ggplot(e_inf, aes(x = e_inf[,2], y = e_inf[,3], color = Influence,
                  label=rownames(e_inf))) +
  stat_poly_line(color="darkgrey", size = 1) +
  stat_poly_eq(use_label(c("R2", "p")),
               hjust = -.5,
               size = 6) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "black", high = "orange",
                       breaks = c(min_inf, mid_inf, 2.45),
                       labels = c(min_inf, mid_inf, 2.45)) +   
  geom_text_repel(size=6) +
  labs(title = "All samples") +
    theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    text = element_text(color = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 20, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.line = element_line(color="black", size = 1),
    axis.ticks = element_line(color="black", size = 1),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1)),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16),
    plot.background = element_blank(),
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text.x = element_text(size=16),
    legend.title=element_text(size=16),
    legend.text=element_text(size=16),
    legend.key=element_blank())
    
e2 <- ggplot(e_drop, aes(x = e_drop[,2], y = e_drop[,3], color = Influence,
                   label = rownames(e_drop))) +
  stat_poly_line(color="darkgrey", size = 1) +
  stat_poly_eq(use_label(c("R2", "p")),
               hjust = -.5,
               size = 6) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "black", high = "orange",
                       breaks = c(min_drop, mid_drop, max_drop),
                       labels = c(min_drop, mid_drop, max_drop)) +
  geom_text_repel(size=6) +
  labs(title = "Dropped highest influence point") +
    theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    text = element_text(color = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 20, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.line = element_line(color="black", size = 1),
    axis.ticks = element_line(color="black", size = 1),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1)),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16),
    plot.background = element_blank(),
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text.x = element_text(size=16),
    legend.title=element_text(size=16),
    legend.text=element_text(size=16),
    legend.key=element_blank())


fig <- ggarrange(e1, e2,
                 labels = c("A", "B"),
                 nrow = 1, ncol = 2)

annotate_figure(fig,
                top = text_grob(names(df_e[3]),
                                size = 22, face = "bold.italic"),
                left = text_grob("Change in abundance",
                                 rot = 90,
                                 size = 20,
                                 face = "bold"),
                bottom = text_grob("Change in Extraversion",
                                   size = 20, face = "bold"))

#ggsave("figures/extra2_genus_influence.png", width = 14, height = 7, units = "in")
```


### Genus: Agreeableness
```{r}
# Agreeableness: empty
df_a <- rchange_filt[colnames(rchange_filt)[colnames(rchange_filt) %in% colnames(sig_a)]]
df_a <- cbind(A_Change = psy_change$A_Change, df_a)
```

### Genus: Neuroticism
```{r}
# Neuroticism: 
df_n <- rchange_filt[colnames(rchange_filt)[colnames(rchange_filt) %in% colnames(sig_n)]]
df_n <- cbind(N_Change = psy_change$N_Change, df_n)

# column 2
m <- lm(df_n[,1] ~ df_n[,2], data=df_n)
summary(m)
dffits(m)
df_fits <- as.data.frame(dffits(m))
colnames(df_fits) <- "Influence"
#find number of predictors in model
p <- length(m$coefficients)-1
#find number of observations
n <- nrow(df_n)
#calculate DFFITS threshold value
thresh <- 2*sqrt(p/n)
#plot DFFITS values for each observation
plot(dffits(m), type = 'h')
abline(h = thresh, lty = 2)
abline(h = -thresh, lty = 2)
# make df with influence
n_inf <- cbind(df_fits,
               "N_Change" = df_n$N_Change,
               "Collinsella" = df_n$Collinsella)
n_inf$Influence <- abs(n_inf$Influence)

n_drop <- n_inf[-which(n_inf$Influence == max(n_inf$Influence)),]

# Custom legend stuff
min_inf <- round(min(n_inf$Influence),2)
max_inf <- round(max(n_inf$Influence),2)
mid_inf <- round((max_inf-min_inf)/2,2)

min_drop <- round(min(n_drop$Influence),2)
max_drop <- round(max(n_drop$Influence),2)
mid_drop <- round((max_drop-min_drop)/2, 2)

ggplot(n_inf, aes(x = n_inf[,3], y = n_inf[,2], color = Influence,
                  label=rownames(n_inf))) +
  stat_poly_line(color="darkgrey", size = 1) +
  stat_poly_eq(use_label(c("R2")),
               hjust = -.5,
               size = 6) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "black", high = "orange",
                       breaks = c(min_inf, mid_inf, max_inf),
                       labels = c(min_inf, mid_inf, max_inf)) +   
  geom_text_repel(size=6) +
  labs(title = names(df_n)[2],
       x = "Change in abundance",
       y = "Change in Neuroticism") +
    theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    text = element_text(color = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 20, hjust = 0.5,
                              face="italic"),
    axis.line = element_line(color="black", size = 1),
    axis.ticks = element_line(color="black", size = 1),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1)),
    axis.title.y = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 2, size=18),
    axis.title.x = element_text(vjust = -0.2, size = 18),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16),
    plot.background = element_blank(),
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text.x = element_text(size=16),
    legend.title=element_text(size=16),
    legend.text=element_text(size=16),
    legend.key=element_blank())
#ggsave("figures/neuroticism_genus1_collinsella.png", width = 10, height = 8, units = "in")


n1 <- ggplot(n_inf, aes(x = n_inf[,2], y = n_inf[,3], color = Influence,
                  label=rownames(n_inf))) +
  stat_poly_line(color="darkgrey", size = 1) +
  stat_poly_eq(use_label(c("R2", "p")),
               hjust = -.5,
               size = 6) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "black", high = "orange",
                       breaks = c(min_inf, mid_inf, max_inf),
                       labels = c(min_inf, mid_inf, max_inf)) +   
  geom_text_repel(size=6) +
  labs(title = "All samples") +
    theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    text = element_text(color = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 20, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.line = element_line(color="black", size = 1),
    axis.ticks = element_line(color="black", size = 1),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1)),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16),
    plot.background = element_blank(),
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text.x = element_text(size=16),
    legend.title=element_text(size=16),
    legend.text=element_text(size=16),
    legend.key=element_blank())
    
n2 <- ggplot(n_drop, aes(x = n_drop[,2], y = n_drop[,3], color = Influence,
                   label = rownames(n_drop))) +
  stat_poly_line(color="darkgrey", size = 1) +
  stat_poly_eq(use_label(c("R2", "p")),
               hjust = -.5,
               size = 6) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "black", high = "orange",
                       breaks = c(min_drop, mid_drop, .56),
                       labels = c(min_drop, mid_drop, .56)) +
  geom_text_repel(size=6) +
  labs(title = "Dropped highest influence point") +
    theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    text = element_text(color = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 20, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.line = element_line(color="black", size = 1),
    axis.ticks = element_line(color="black", size = 1),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1)),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16),
    plot.background = element_blank(),
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text.x = element_text(size=16),
    legend.title=element_text(size=16),
    legend.text=element_text(size=16),
    legend.key=element_blank())


fig <- ggarrange(n1, n2,
                 labels = c("A", "B"),
                 nrow = 1, ncol = 2)

annotate_figure(fig,
                top = text_grob(names(df_n[2]),
                                size = 22, face = "bold.italic"),
                left = text_grob("Change in abundance",
                                 rot = 90,
                                 size = 20,
                                 face = "bold"),
                bottom = text_grob("Change in Neuroticism",
                                   size = 20, face = "bold"))

#ggsave("figures/neuroticism_genus_influence.png", width = 14, height = 7, units = "in")
```


## Family analysis
```{r}
# copy-pasted from above
# get change in psychological scores
df <- read_q2metadata("data/aya_meta_costarica.tsv")
df <- filter(df, Consumed_Aya == "yes")

# FFM (OCEAN)
df$O_Change <- NA # openness
df$C_Change <- NA # conscientiousness
df$E_Change <- NA # extraversion
df$A_Change <- NA # agreeableness
df$N_Change <- NA # neuroticism

# PCL5 (PTSD score)
df$PCL_Change <- NA

evens <- seq(2,26, by = 2)
for (x in evens){
  o_change <- df$Openness[x] - df$Openness[x-1]
  df$O_Change[x-1] <- o_change
  df$O_Change[x] <- o_change
  
  c_change <- df$Conscientiousness[x] - df$Conscientiousness[x-1]
  df$C_Change[x-1] <- c_change
  df$C_Change[x] <- c_change
  
  e_change <- df$Extraversion[x] - df$Extraversion[x-1]
  df$E_Change[x-1] <- e_change
  df$E_Change[x] <- e_change
  
  a_change <- df$Agreeableness[x] - df$Agreeableness[x-1]
  df$A_Change[x-1] <- a_change
  df$A_Change[x] <- a_change
  
  n_change <- df$Neuroticism[x] - df$Neuroticism[x-1]
  df$N_Change[x-1] <- n_change
  df$N_Change[x] <- n_change
  
  p_change <- df$PCL5_sum_1_2[x] - df$PCL5_sum_1_2[x-1]
  df$PCL_Change[x-1] <- p_change
  df$PCL_Change[x] <- p_change
}

# pull out T1 so there aren't double entries
meta <- dplyr::filter(df, Timepoint == 1)


# FAMILY linear regression
rarefied_table <- read_qza("core-diversity-results-cr/rarefied_table.qza")$data
r_df <- as.data.frame(rarefied_table)
taxonomy <- read_qza("data/taxonomy-silva.qza")$data %>% parse_taxonomy()

# using the ASV rownames, match genera to ASVs
r_df$Family <- taxonomy[match(row.names(r_df), row.names(taxonomy)),"Family"]
r_df$Family <- as.factor(r_df$Family)

# add rows based on column
r_df <- ddply(r_df, "Family", numcolwise(sum))
r_df <- dplyr::filter(r_df, is.na(Family) == FALSE)

# clean up
rdf <- dplyr::select(r_df, -Family)
rdf <- t(rdf)
rdf <- as.data.frame(rdf)
colnames(rdf) <- r_df$Family

# drop low variance columns
zeros_count <- colSums(rdf == 0)
to_drop <- names(zeros_count[zeros_count > 24])
rdf2 <- rdf[, !(names(rdf)) %in% to_drop]

# copy-pasted from above
rare_change <- rdf2
rare_change[rare_change >= 0] <- NA

# calculate change in abundance between t1 and t2
for (col in 1:ncol(rare_change)){
  for (x in evens){
    tax_change <- rdf2[,col][x] - rdf2[,col][x-1]
    rare_change[,col][x-1] <- tax_change
    rare_change[,col][x] <- tax_change
  }
}

rare_change$Timepoint <- rep(c(1,2), 13)
# keep only t1 so not double entries
rare_change <- dplyr::filter(rare_change, Timepoint == 1)

# drop subject 17 from both dataframes due to missing data
rare_change$ID <- rownames(rare_change)
rare_change$Subject <- substr(rare_change$ID, start = 1, stop = 2)
rare_change$Subject <- as.factor(rare_change$Subject)

meta_filt <- filter(meta, Subject_Num != "17")
rchange_filt <- filter(rare_change, Subject != "17")
# select() use will conflict with MASS package unless dplyr is specified
rchange_filt <- dplyr::select(rchange_filt, -Timepoint, -ID, -Subject)

# dfs for p values of cor.test
cor_o <- rchange_filt[1,]
cor_c <- rchange_filt[1,]
cor_e <- rchange_filt[1,]
cor_a <- rchange_filt[1,]
cor_n <- rchange_filt[1,]
cor_p <- rchange_filt[1,]
psy_change <- meta_filt[,78:83]
rownames(psy_change) <- meta_filt$SampleID

# for each psych measure, and for each taxa, make df with stats results
# this has 5970 loops
fail_check <- 0
for (psy in 1:ncol(psy_change)){
  col_name <- colnames(psy_change[psy])
  for (tax in 1:ncol(rchange_filt)){
    p <- cor.test(psy_change[,psy], rchange_filt[,tax], method="pearson")$p.value
    ifelse(col_name == "O_Change",
           cor_o[,tax] <- p,
    ifelse(col_name == "C_Change",
           cor_c[,tax] <- p,
    ifelse(col_name == "E_Change",
           cor_e[,tax] <- p,
    ifelse(col_name == "A_Change",
           cor_a[,tax] <- p,
    ifelse(col_name == "N_Change",
           cor_n[,tax] <- p,
    ifelse(col_name == "PCL_Change",
           cor_p[,tax] <- p,
           fail_check + 1))))))
  }
}

# drop NAs caused by 0 standard deviation
cor_o <- cor_o[, colSums(is.na(cor_o)) == 0]
cor_c <- cor_c[, colSums(is.na(cor_c)) == 0]
cor_e <- cor_e[, colSums(is.na(cor_e)) == 0]
cor_a <- cor_a[, colSums(is.na(cor_a)) == 0]
cor_n <- cor_n[, colSums(is.na(cor_n)) == 0]
cor_p <- cor_p[, colSums(is.na(cor_p)) == 0]

# adjust for multiple comparisons? obliterates significance
q_o <- apply(cor_o, 2, function(x) p.adjust(x, method = "fdr", n = ncol(cor_o)))
q_c <- apply(cor_c, 2, function(x) p.adjust(x, method = "fdr", n = ncol(cor_c)))
q_e <- apply(cor_e, 2, function(x) p.adjust(x, method = "fdr", n = ncol(cor_e)))
q_a <- apply(cor_a, 2, function(x) p.adjust(x, method = "fdr", n = ncol(cor_a)))
q_n <- apply(cor_n, 2, function(x) p.adjust(x, method = "fdr", n = ncol(cor_n)))
q_p <- apply(cor_p, 2, function(x) p.adjust(x, method = "fdr", n = ncol(cor_p)))
qdf_o <- as.data.frame(q_o)
qdf_c <- as.data.frame(q_c)
qdf_e <- as.data.frame(q_e)
qdf_a <- as.data.frame(q_a)
qdf_n <- as.data.frame(q_n)
qdf_p <- as.data.frame(q_p)

# filter if p <.01
sig_o <- cor_o %>% keep(~ any(. <= .01))
sig_c <- cor_c %>% keep(~ any(. <= .01))
sig_e <- cor_e %>% keep(~ any(. <= .01))
sig_a <- cor_a %>% keep(~ any(. <= .01))
sig_n <- cor_n %>% keep(~ any(. <= .01))
sig_p <- cor_p %>% keep(~ any(. <= .01))
```

#### Family: Extraversion
```{r}
# check linearity
# Extraversion
df_e <- rchange_filt[colnames(rchange_filt)[colnames(rchange_filt) %in% colnames(sig_e)]]
df_e <- cbind(E_Change = psy_change$E_Change, df_e)
plot(df_e[,1], df_e[,3])

# 3 looks linear
m <- lm(df_e[,1] ~ df_e[,3], data=df_e)
summary(m)
dffits(m)
df_fits <- as.data.frame(dffits(m))
colnames(df_fits) <- "Influence"
#find number of predictors in model
p <- length(m$coefficients)-1
#find number of observations
n <- nrow(df_e)
#calculate DFFITS threshold value
thresh <- 2*sqrt(p/n)
#plot DFFITS values for each observation
plot(dffits(m), type = 'h')
abline(h = thresh, lty = 2)
abline(h = -thresh, lty = 2)
# make df with influence
e_inf <- cbind(df_fits,
               "E_Change" = df_e$E_Change,
               "Christensenellaceae" = df_e$Christensenellaceae)
e_inf$Influence <- abs(e_inf$Influence)

e_inf <- e_inf[-4,]

# col 3 is no longer significant or sloped either
ggplot(e_inf, aes(x = e_inf[,1], y = e_inf[,3], color=Influence)) +
  stat_poly_line() +
  stat_poly_eq(use_label(c("R2", "p")),
               size = 6) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(title = names(df_e)[3]) +
    theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    text = element_text(colour = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "italic", 
                              size = 17, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.line = element_line(colour="black", size = 1),
    axis.ticks = element_line(colour="black", size = 1),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1)),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16),
    plot.background = element_blank(),
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text.x = element_text(size=16),
    legend.title=element_text(size=16),
    legend.text=element_text(size=16),
    legend.key=element_blank())

```



### Family: Neuroticism
```{r}
# Neuroticism: 
df_n <- rchange_filt[colnames(rchange_filt)[colnames(rchange_filt) %in% colnames(sig_n)]]
df_n <- cbind(N_Change = psy_change$N_Change, df_n)
plot(df_n[,1], df_n[,2])

# column 2
m <- lm(df_n[,1] ~ df_n[,2], data=df_n)
summary(m)
dffits(m)
df_fits <- as.data.frame(dffits(m))
colnames(df_fits) <- "Influence"
#find number of predictors in model
p <- length(m$coefficients)-1
#find number of observations
n <- nrow(df_e)
#calculate DFFITS threshold value
thresh <- 2*sqrt(p/n)
#plot DFFITS values for each observation
plot(dffits(m), type = 'h')
abline(h = thresh, lty = 2)
abline(h = -thresh, lty = 2)
# make df with influence
n_inf <- cbind(df_fits,
               "N_Change" = df_n$N_Change,
               "Coriobacteriaceae" = df_n$Coriobacteriaceae)
n_inf$Influence <- abs(n_inf$Influence)

n_inf <- n_inf[-1,]

# Neuroticism plot
ggplot(n_inf, aes(x = n_inf[,2], y = n_inf[,3], color=Influence)) +
  stat_poly_line(color = "darkgrey", size = 1) +
  stat_poly_eq(use_label(c("R2", "p")),
               hjust = -.5,
               size = 6) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "black", high = "orange") +
  labs(title = names(df_n)[2],
       x = "Change in Neuroticism",
       y = "Change in abundance") +
    theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    text = element_text(colour = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 20, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.line = element_line(colour="black", size = 1),
    axis.ticks = element_line(colour="black", size = 1),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1)),
    axis.title.y = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 2, size=18),
    axis.title.x = element_text(vjust = -0.2, size = 18),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16),
    plot.background = element_blank(),
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text.x = element_text(size=16),
    legend.title=element_text(size=16),
    legend.text=element_text(size=16),
    legend.key=element_blank())

#ggsave("figures/neuro_family_cor.png", width = 8, height = 6, units = "in")

```


# PhILR
Package By Justin Silverman: https://bioconductor.org/packages/release/bioc/vignettes/philr/inst/doc/philr-intro.html
PhILR is short for “Phylogenetic Isometric Log-Ratio Transform.” This determines the parts of a phylogenetic tree that relate to your outcome, AKA ”balance tree analysis.

Code help: John Sterrett
https://github.com/sterrettJD/Phadtare-IBS/blob/main/PhILR.Rmd

```{r}
pseq <- qiime2R::qza_to_phyloseq(features="data/cr-table.qza",
                         taxonomy="data/taxonomy-silva.qza",
                         tree="data/insertion-tree.qza",
                         metadata="data/aya_meta_costarica.tsv")

# Filter low-abundance ASVs
orig.asvs <- nrow(otu_table(pseq))
# keep only the ASVs found in more than 10% of samples

pseq.filt <- filter_taxa(pseq, 
                         function(x) sum(x>0) > (0.1*length(x)), 
                         prune=T
                         )
presence.filt.remaining <- nrow(otu_table(pseq.filt))

# keep only the ASVs that we have at least 100 reads of
pseq.filt <- filter_taxa(pseq.filt, 
                         function(x) sum(x) > 100, 
                         prune=T
                         )
phylo.filt.final.remaining <- nrow(otu_table(pseq.filt))

# filter taxa that aren't very variable
pseq.filt <- filter_taxa(pseq.filt, 
                         function(x) sd(x)/mean(x) > 3.0, 
                         prune=T
                         )

phylo.filt.final.remaining.variable <- nrow(otu_table(pseq.filt))

cat(paste("Original features:", orig.asvs,
            "\nAfter first filtering step:", presence.filt.remaining,
            "\nAfter second filtering step:", phylo.filt.final.remaining,
            "\nAfter third (variability) filtering step:", phylo.filt.final.remaining.variable))

pseq.filt # check n features

# add pseudocount
temp.tab <- otu_table(pseq.filt) %>% as.data.frame()
temp.tab[temp.tab==0] <- 1
otu_table(pseq.filt) <- otu_table(temp.tab, taxa_are_rows=T)

# check there are no zeros
head(otu_table(pseq.filt))

# Process tree
if(ape::is.rooted(phy_tree(pseq.filt)) == T){
    "Tree is rooted."
} else {
    "TREE IS NOT ROOTED"
}

if(ape::is.binary(phy_tree(pseq.filt)) == T){
    "Tree is binary."
} else {
    "TREE IS NOT BINARY"
}

# rename nodes to be "n1, n2, ..."
tree <- ape::makeNodeLabel(phy_tree(pseq.filt), method="number", prefix='n')
phy_tree(pseq.filt) <- tree

# name a balance to check
name.balance(tree, tax_table(pseq.filt), 'n58', return.votes = "up")

# look at asvs
descendants <- extract.clade(tree, 'n58')$tip.label
descendants

# create philr object
philr.dat <- philr(otu_table(pseq.filt) %>% t(), # have to transpose the otu table
                  phy_tree(pseq.filt),
                  part.weights='enorm.x.gm.counts', 
                  ilr.weights='blw.sqrt')

```


## LASSO
Run code above first.
Assess balances of PhILR results that associate with the outcome of interest.
New packages: glmnet, ggtree

```{r}
metadata <- sample_data(pseq.filt) %>% as.data.frame()
metadata$Timepoint <- as.double(metadata$Timepoint)

glmmod <- glmnet(x=philr.dat,
                 y=metadata$Timepoint,
                 alpha=1)

# s would need to be switched to 3000 if y is large
# can adjust s until there are 3-10 top results
top.coords <- as.matrix(coef(glmmod, s=1/9))
top.coords <- top.coords[2:length(top.coords),]

top.coords <- top.coords[order(abs(top.coords), decreasing=T)]
top.coords <- top.coords[top.coords != 0]
top.coords.values <- top.coords
top.coords <- names(top.coords)


# get summary stats for selected features regression
m.formula <- paste(top.coords, collapse=" + ")
m.formula <- paste0("metadata$Timepoint ~ ", m.formula)
m <- lm(as.formula(m.formula),
        data=as.data.frame(philr.dat))
summary(m)

# p <- summary(m)$coefficients[,4]
# nodes <- rownames(summary(m)$coefficients)
# np_df <- data.frame("nodes" = nodes,
#                     "p.value" = p)
# np_top <- filter(np_df, p < .05)

tc.names <- sapply(top.coords, function(x) name.balance(phy_tree(pseq.filt), tax_table(pseq.filt), x))

```



## Get balance details
Code by John Sterrett (Github: sterrettJD)
https://github.com/sterrettJD/Phadtare-IBS/blob/a44d17c0ad8e9c0657bf8c3bc48e4d14d5cad5d2/PhILR.Rmd#L195

```{r}
asv.node <- philr(otu_table(pseq.filt) %>% t(), # have to transpose the otu table
                  phy_tree(pseq.filt),
                  part.weights='enorm.x.gm.counts', 
                  ilr.weights='blw.sqrt',return.all=T)$sbp


tax.table <- tax_table(pseq.filt) %>% as.data.frame()


make_num_summary <- function(curr.node){
    num <- this.node[this.node==1]
    num.asvs <- names(num)
    
    tax.table[num.asvs,] %>% 
        as.data.table() %>% 
        mutate_all(as.factor) %>% 
        summary() %>% print()
}


make_num_full <- function(curr.node){
    num <- this.node[this.node==1]
    num.asvs <- names(num)
    
    tax.table[num.asvs,] %>% print()
}


make_denom_summary <- function(curr.node){
    denom <- this.node[this.node==-1]
    denom.asvs <- names(denom)
    
    tax.table[denom.asvs,] %>% 
        as.data.table() %>% 
        mutate_all(as.factor) %>% 
        summary() %>% print()
}

make_denom_full <- function(curr.node){
    denom <- this.node[this.node==-1]
    denom.asvs <- names(denom)
    
    tax.table[denom.asvs,] %>% print()
}


top.coords

c <- "n69"
this.node <- asv.node[,c]
num69 <- make_num_full(this.node)
num69$Ratio <- "Numerator"
den69 <- make_denom_full(this.node)
den69$Ratio <- "Denominator"
df69 <- rbind(num69, den69)
df69$Node <- "69"

c <- "n28"
this.node <- asv.node[,c]
num28 <- make_num_full(this.node)
num28$Ratio <- "Numerator"
den28 <- make_denom_full(this.node)
den28$Ratio <- "Denominator"
df28 <- rbind(num28, den28)
df28$Node <- "28"

c <- "n70"
this.node <- asv.node[,c]
num70 <- make_num_full(this.node)
num70$Ratio <- "Numerator"
den70 <- make_denom_full(this.node)
den70$Ratio <- "Denominator"
df70 <- rbind(num70, den70)
df70$Node <- "70"

c <- "n58"
this.node <- asv.node[,c]
num58 <- make_num_full(this.node)
num58$Ratio <- "Numerator"
den58 <- make_denom_full(this.node)
den58$Ratio <- "Denominator"
df58 <- rbind(num58, den58)
df58$Node <- "58"

c <- "n76"
this.node <- asv.node[,c]
num76 <- make_num_full(this.node)
num76$Ratio <- "Numerator"
den76 <- make_denom_full(this.node)
den76$Ratio <- "Denominator"
df76 <- rbind(num76, den76)
df76$Node <- "76"


df_nodes <- rbind(df28, df58, df69, df70, df76)
df_nodes$ASV <- rownames(df_nodes)

write.csv(df_nodes, file = "data/philr_asvs.csv", row.names = F)

```



## Tree
```{r}
tc.nn <- name.to.nn(phy_tree(pseq.filt), top.coords)

tc.colors <- c('#fb9a99',  '#b2df8a', '#33a02c', '#1f78b4', '#a6cee3')

  
  # geom_cladelabel(node = as.numeric(gsub(".*?([0-9]+).*", 
  #                                        "\\1", 
  #                                        top.coords[1])) ,
  #                 label = top.coords[1],
  #                 color = "black",
  #                 offset = 0,
  #                 align = TRUE)


p <- ggtree(tree) +
    geom_balance(node=tc.nn[1], fill=tc.colors[1], alpha=0.6) +
    geom_balance(node=tc.nn[2], fill=tc.colors[2], alpha=0.6) +
    geom_balance(node=tc.nn[3], fill=tc.colors[3], alpha=0.6) +
    geom_balance(node=tc.nn[4], fill=tc.colors[4], alpha=0.6) +
    geom_balance(node=tc.nn[5], fill=tc.colors[5], alpha=0.6) 


p <- annotate_balance(tr=phy_tree(pseq.filt), coord=top.coords[1],
                 p=p,
                 labels=paste(rep(top.coords[1],2), c("num", "denom")),
                 offset.text=0.01,
                 bar=FALSE,
                 hjust=0,
                 size=3) +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = 0.3))


p <- annotate_balance(tr=phy_tree(pseq.filt), coord=top.coords[2], 
                 p=p, 
                 labels=paste(rep(top.coords[2],2), c("num", "denom")),
                 offset.text=0.01, bar=FALSE,
                 hjust=0,
                 vjust=.5,
                 size=3)


p <- annotate_balance(tr=phy_tree(pseq.filt), coord=top.coords[3], 
                 p=p,
                 labels=paste(rep(top.coords[3],2), c("num", "denom")),
                 offset.text=.01, bar=FALSE,
                 hjust=0,
                 vjust=-.2,
                 size=3)


p <- annotate_balance(tr=phy_tree(pseq.filt), coord=top.coords[4], 
                 p=p, 
                 #labels=paste(rep(top.coords[4],2), c("num", "denom")),
                 labels=paste(rep(top.coords[4],2), c("num", "denom")),
                 offset.text=0.01, bar=FALSE,
                 hjust=0,
                 vjust=.5,
                 size=3)


p <- annotate_balance(tr=phy_tree(pseq.filt), coord=top.coords[5], 
                 p=p, 
                 labels=paste(rep(top.coords[5],2), c("num", "denom")),
                 offset.text=.01, bar=FALSE,
                 hjust=0,
                 size=3)


p

#ggsave("figures/tree_PhILR.svg", height=10, width=8)
```

## Spaghetti graphs
```{r, fig.height=10, fig.width=14}
philr.long <- convert_to_long(philr.dat, 
                              labels=metadata$Timepoint) %>%
  filter(coord %in% top.coords)

philr.long$coord <- as.factor(philr.long$coord) 
philr.long$labels <- as.factor(philr.long$labels)

philr.long$labels <- fct_recode(philr.long$labels,
                                "Before" = "1",
                                "After" = "2")

philr.long$sample <- as.factor(philr.long$sample)

metadata$ID <- rownames(metadata)
philr.long$subject <- metadata$Subject_Num[match(philr.long$sample, metadata$ID)]

scatter.labels <- paste(names(tc.names), tc.names, sep=": ")
names(scatter.labels) <- names(tc.names)

ggplot(philr.long, aes(x=labels, y=value, color=coord, group=subject)) +
  #geom_violin(draw_quantiles = c(0.25, 0.5, 0.75)) +
  #geom_boxplot()+
  geom_point(position="jitter")+
  #geom_point()+
  #geom_line()+
  scale_color_manual(guide="none", values = c('#fb9a99',  '#b2df8a', '#33a02c', '#1f78b4', '#a6cee3')) +
  facet_wrap(.~coord,
             scales='free', ncol=1,
             labeller=labeller(coord=scatter.labels)) +
  labs(x = 'Timepoint', y = 'Balance Value') +
 # scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::comma) +
  theme_bw()


#philr.long$labels <- as.numeric(as.character(philr.long$labels))



# ggarrange(p, p2,
#           labels = c("A", "B"),
#           font.label = list(size = 18),
#           nrow = 1, ncol = 2)


#ggsave("figures/timepoint_PhILR.svg", height=10, width=14)
```

### Individual spaghetti
Run code above first
```{r}
philr.long$timepoint <- rep(c(1,2), 65)
philr.long$timepoint <- as.factor(philr.long$timepoint)

philr.short <- pivot_wider(philr.long,
                           names_from = coord,
                           values_from=value)
ps1 <- filter(philr.short, timepoint=="1")
ps2 <- filter(philr.short, timepoint=="2")

# fix names based on what all numerator or denominator ASVs shared
tc.names
# n28 is fine


# n58
name.balance(tree, tax_table(pseq.filt), 'n58', return.votes = "up")
tc.names[4] <- "Species_uncultured_Flavonifractor/Genus_Flavonifractor" 

# n70
name.balance(tree, tax_table(pseq.filt), 'n70', return.votes = "up")
tc.names[3] <- "Species_uncultured_Christensenellaceae_R7/Class_Clostridia" 

# n69
name.balance(tree, tax_table(pseq.filt), 'n69', return.votes = "down")
tc.names[1] <- "Genus_Veillonella/Species_uncultured_Megasphaera"

# n76
name.balance(tree, tax_table(pseq.filt), 'n76', return.votes = "up")
tc.names[5] <- "Species_uncultured_Parasutterella/Class_Gammaproteobacteria" 

n28 <- ggplot(philr.short, aes(x=labels, y=n28, group=subject)) +
  geom_point()+
  geom_line(color='#b2df8a')+
  geom_segment(aes(x = .8, y = mean(ps1$n28),
                   xend = 2.2, yend = mean(ps2$n28)),
               color="black",
               size = 1,
               linetype =2) +
  labs(title = (paste(names(tc.names[2]), tc.names[2], sep=": ")))+
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.line = element_line(colour = "black"),
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
    axis.title.x=element_blank(),
    axis.title.y=element_blank())


n58 <- ggplot(philr.short, aes(x=labels, y=n58, group=subject)) +
  geom_point()+
  geom_line(color='#1f78b4')+
  geom_segment(aes(x = .8, y = mean(ps1$n58),
                   xend = 2.2, yend = mean(ps2$n58)),
               color="black",
               size = 1,
               linetype =2) +
  labs(title = (paste(names(tc.names[4]), tc.names[4], sep=": ")))+
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.line = element_line(colour = "black"),
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
    axis.title.x=element_blank(),
    axis.title.y=element_blank())


n69 <- ggplot(philr.short, aes(x=labels, y=n69, group=subject)) +
  geom_point()+
  geom_line(color='#fb9a99')+
  geom_segment(aes(x = .8, y = mean(ps1$n69),
                   xend = 2.2, yend = mean(ps2$n69)),
               color="black",
               size = 1,
               linetype =2) +
  labs(title = (paste(names(tc.names[1]), tc.names[1], sep=": ")))+
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.line = element_line(colour = "black"),
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
    axis.title.x=element_blank(),
    axis.title.y=element_blank())


n70 <- ggplot(philr.short, aes(x=labels, y=n70, group=subject)) +
  geom_point()+
  geom_line(color='#33a02c')+
  geom_segment(aes(x = .8, y = mean(ps1$n70),
                   xend = 2.2, yend = mean(ps2$n70)),
               color="black",
               size = 1,
               linetype =2) +
  labs(title = (paste(names(tc.names[3]), tc.names[3], sep=": ")))+
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.line = element_line(colour = "black"),
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
    axis.title.x=element_blank(),
    axis.title.y=element_blank())


n76 <- ggplot(philr.short, aes(x=labels, y=n76, group=subject)) +
  geom_point()+
  geom_line(color='#a6cee3')+
  geom_segment(aes(x = .8, y = mean(ps1$n76),
                   xend = 2.2, yend = mean(ps2$n76)),
               color="black",
               size = 1,
               linetype =2) +
  labs(title = (paste(names(tc.names[5]), tc.names[5], sep=": ")))+
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.line = element_line(colour = "black"),
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
    axis.title.x=element_blank(),
    axis.title.y=element_blank())


  # theme(axis.line = element_line(colour = "black"),
  #   panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
  #   panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
  #   panel.border = element_blank(),
  #   panel.background = element_blank(),
  #   plot.title = element_text(face="bold", size=15, hjust = 0.5),
  #   axis.title.x=element_blank(),
  #   axis.text = element_text(size = 13),
  #   axis.text.x = element_text(size = 14),
  #   axis.text.y = element_text(size = 14),
  #   axis.title.y=element_blank())

fig <- ggarrange(n28, n58, n70, n69, n76,
                 nrow = 5, ncol = 1)

spag <- annotate_figure(fig,
                left = text_grob("Balance Value", 
                                 rot = 90, size = 16, face = "bold"),
                bottom = text_grob("Timepoint", size = 16, face = "bold"))
spag

#ggsave("figures/node_change_PhILR.svg", height=10, width=8)

ggarrange(p, spag,
          labels = c("A", "B"),
          font.label = list(size = 18),
          nrow = 1, ncol = 2)


#ggsave("figures/PhILR.svg", height=10, width=14)

```


# Summarize all results
Make a table with counts per taxa 
```{r}
df <- read_csv("data/Summary_table.csv")

pre <- filter(df, Condition == "Before")
post <- filter(df, Condition == "After")

taxon <- c("Phylum", "Class", "Order", "Family", "Genus", "Species")

# b = before
phy_b <- count(pre$Phylum)
phy_b$Taxon <- "Phylum"
cla_b <- count(pre$Class)
cla_b$Taxon <- "Class"
ord_b <- count(pre$Order)
ord_b$Taxon <- "Order"
fam_b <- count(pre$Family)
fam_b$Taxon <- "Family"
gen_b <- count(pre$Genus)
gen_b$Taxon <- "Genus"
spe_b <- count(pre$Species)
spe_b$Taxon <- "Species"
pre_sum <- rbind(phy_b, cla_b, ord_b,
                 fam_b, gen_b, spe_b)
pre_sum$Condition <- "Before"

# a = after
phy_a <- count(post$Phylum)
phy_a$Taxon <- "Phylum"
cla_a <- count(post$Class)
cla_a$Taxon <- "Class"
ord_a <- count(pre$Order)
ord_a$Taxon <- "Order"
fam_a <- count(pre$Family)
fam_a$Taxon <- "Family"
gen_a <- count(pre$Genus)
gen_a$Taxon <- "Genus"
spe_a <- count(pre$Species)
spe_a$Taxon <- "Species"
post_sum <- rbind(phy_a, cla_a, ord_a,
                 fam_a, gen_a, spe_a)
post_sum$Condition <- "After"

sum_df <- rbind(pre_sum, post_sum)

sum_df <- sum_df[, c(3, 4, 1, 2)]
colnames(sum_df)[3] <- "Name"
colnames(sum_df)[4] <- "Frequency"

write.csv(sum_df, "data/name_counts_summary.csv", row.names=F)
```


# End of Line
